🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.21-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 4.21 | Total failures: 15



Cross-Suite Summary


TL;DR: A linuxptp-daemon regression blocks all slave sync-state detection, timing out 13/15 ClockSync tests across every suite.



Root Cause Groups


• 13/15 failures — GetProfileLogID / log-stream timeout blocking ClockSync verification (suites: bc ×3, dualfollower ×3, dualnicbc ×3, dualnicbcha ×2, oc ×2)

Every "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", and "Can provide a profile with higher priority" test hits the same ~207–210 s timeout. Two overlapping mechanisms contribute:


1. liveGate startup drain (dualfollower / all slave suites): The new liveGate mechanism in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go blocks the socket-writer goroutine for up to 60 s after each profile apply and then discards buffered ptp4l lines. EmitClockSyncLogs() only replays state for T-GM nodes, so slave/follower sync-state is invisible to the test framework until a fresh live line arrives — which never happens within the GetProfileLogID 3-minute window.
2. Pod log stream closed with context deadline exceeded (~60–70 s, dualnicbc / bc): The Follow: true Kubernetes log stream is cut by network infrastructure (HAProxy / kube-apiserver ~60 s idle timeout in OCP 4.21 nightly), causing both retry passes of GetProfileLogID to fail before the Eventually expires.

Both mechanisms trace to the same upstream sync merged the day before the run (linuxptp-daemon PR #613, commit 0459bc0, Jun 23 2026).


• 2/15 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL never sustains clock-class 7 holdover on GNSS loss (suite: tgm ×2)

In both TGM holdover tests, the DPLL on cnfdf32 either never reaches class 7 (Test 1: 47+ retries, 197 s timeout) or exits class-7 immediately to class 248/FREERUN (Test 2). Root cause is an overly aggressive maxInSpecOffset (20 ns) in HardwareConfig, causing the in-spec holdover timer to collapse to ~192 s and the DPLL to transition directly to FREERUN before the test verifies it. This is isolated to the tgm suite and the WPC NIC hardware on that node.



Cross-Suite Patterns


SuiteFailuresRoot Cause
bc3/3GetProfileLogID timeout (operator-pod-deletion restarts daemon mid-test)
dualfollower3/3liveGate drains buffered lines; slave state invisible
dualnicbc3/3Log stream closed context deadline exceeded at ~60–70 s
dualnicbcha2/2Same systemic ClockSync timeout (no XML artifact captured)
tgm2/2Suite-specific: DPLL holdover hardware/config regression

Shared: bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha all share the same underlying cause — the linuxptp-daemon liveGate/stream regression introduced in PR #613 prevents GetProfileLogID from ever receiving the Ptp4lConf: #profile: startup log within its timeout window.


Suite-specific: tgm failures are independent — caused by a HardwareConfig holdover parameter misconfiguration (or a DPLL driver regression) on the WPC NIC node; they would persist even if the ClockSync stream issue were fixed.



Actionable Recommendations


1. Revert or fix linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, Jun 23 2026). The liveGate drain-on-reset behavior discards the Ptp4lConf: startup lines that GetProfileLogID depends on. Either: (a) emit a synthetic replay line for slave/follower profiles (matching what EmitClockSyncLogs does for T-GM), or (b) gate the drain only on a confirmed-live line rather than discarding all buffered lines on profile apply. This alone unblocks 13/15 failures.

2. Fix the pod log stream context deadline exceeded disconnects. GetPodLogsRegex should reconnect and resume scanning on stream error rather than propagating the error up through GetProfileLogID. Adding retry-with-reconnect inside findRegexInStream would make the test resilient to the ~60 s kube-apiserver/HAProxy connection timeout independently of the liveGate fix.

3. Investigate the WPC T-GM DPLL holdover configuration. Audit holdoverParameters.maxInSpecOffset in the deployed HardwareConfig for cnfdf32; a value of 20 ns causes the in-spec timer to expire almost immediately. Align it with realistic oscillator specs (e.g., ≥100 ns) or audit the linuxptp-daemon/pkg/dpll/dpll.go holdover-timer formula for a regression introduced alongside PR #613.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 13/15 ClockSync tests fail across every non-tgm suite due to a single code change merged the previous day; the entire periodic 4.21 PTP job is effectively non-functional.




Per-Suite Details



bc


Now I have enough to write the complete analysis. Here it is:



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three bc-serial ClockSync tests fail because GetProfileLogID cannot find the daemon's Ptp4lConf: #profile: startup log within its 3-minute window, most likely because the daemon pods restarted (triggered by the operator-pod-deletion test that ran 9 seconds earlier) and hadn't yet completed initialization when the ClockSync tests began.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Daemon log lookup timeout — GetProfileLogID exhausts its 3-minute search window (affects: all 3 tests)

• Evidence:
• [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master [Serial]: "time": 207.094107648, "result": "error"
• [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]: "time": 206.698651671, "result": "error"
• [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Can provide a profile with higher priority [Serial]: "time": 271.33427173, "result": "error"
• The immediately preceding test logs: [It] [bc-serial] PTP configuration verifications Should re-acquire lease within 5 minutes after operator pod deletion [Serial] passed in 5.23s — this deleted and recreated the ptp-operator pod at ~04:03:09 UTC.
• TimeoutIn3Minutes = 3 * time.Minute (test/pkg/consts.go:50) is the timeout used by GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:81).
• test/pkg/pods/pods.go:232: snapCtx, snapCancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), pkg.TimeoutIn1Minute) — Pass 1 (no-follow existing-log read).
• test/pkg/pods/pods.go:254: ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout) — Pass 2 (follow stream, 3-min limit).
• The bc suite XML header:
• Daemon log rotation baseline: at ~300 bytes/sec over 1h55m ≈ 2 MB, well within the 10 MB CRI-O default — so the startup logs should have been present for the first two tests, but the daemon was mid-restart from the operator reconciliation, so the fresh pod had no buffered Ptp4lConf: entries yet.
• test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:69–93: GetProfileLogID searches for (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: %s(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.*)\] in pod logs; returns error "could not get any profile line" after Pass 2 timeout.
• Timing breakdown: tests 1 & 2 take 207 s ≈ 180 s (Pass 2 timeout) + ~27 s (BeforeEach, findMatchingPod, Pass 1 no-match read). Test 3 takes 271 s ≈ 91 s (WaitForConfigMapProfile in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:386) + 180 s (GetPodLogsRegex waiting for "Profile Name: "+pkg.PtpTempPolicyName).

• Root Cause: BasicClockSyncCheck (test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:32) calls GetClockIDForeign → GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:69), which uses a two-pass GetPodLogsRegex: Pass 1 reads all existing logs (1-minute limit) for Ptp4lConf: #profile: …message_tag [...]; Pass 2 follows the stream for up to 3 minutes. Both pass fail because the linuxptp-daemon pods restarted after the operator reconciliation triggered by the "



Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.21-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 4.21 | Total failures: 15



Cross-Suite Summary


TL;DR: A linuxptp-daemon regression blocks all slave sync-state detection, timing out 13/15 ClockSync tests across every suite.



Root Cause Groups


• 13/15 failures — GetProfileLogID / log-stream timeout blocking ClockSync verification (suites: bc ×3, dualfollower ×3, dualnicbc ×3, dualnicbcha ×2, oc ×2)

Every "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", and "Can provide a profile with higher priority" test hits the same ~207–210 s timeout. Two overlapping mechanisms contribute:


1. liveGate startup drain (dualfollower / all slave suites): The new liveGate mechanism in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go blocks the socket-writer goroutine for up to 60 s after each profile apply and then discards buffered ptp4l lines. EmitClockSyncLogs() only replays state for T-GM nodes, so slave/follower sync-state is invisible to the test framework until a fresh live line arrives — which never happens within the GetProfileLogID 3-minute window.
2. Pod log stream closed with context deadline exceeded (~60–70 s, dualnicbc / bc): The Follow: true Kubernetes log stream is cut by network infrastructure (HAProxy / kube-apiserver ~60 s idle timeout in OCP 4.21 nightly), causing both retry passes of GetProfileLogID to fail before the Eventually expires.

Both mechanisms trace to the same upstream sync merged the day before the run (linuxptp-daemon PR #613, commit 0459bc0, Jun 23 2026).


• 2/15 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL never sustains clock-class 7 holdover on GNSS loss (suite: tgm ×2)

In both TGM holdover tests, the DPLL on cnfdf32 either never reaches class 7 (Test 1: 47+ retries, 197 s timeout) or exits class-7 immediately to class 248/FREERUN (Test 2). Root cause is an overly aggressive maxInSpecOffset (20 ns) in HardwareConfig, causing the in-spec holdover timer to collapse to ~192 s and the DPLL to transition directly to FREERUN before the test verifies it. This is isolated to the tgm suite and the WPC NIC hardware on that node.

Suite-specific: tgm failures are independent — caused by a HardwareConfig holdover parameter misconfiguration (or a DPLL driver regression) on the WPC NIC node; they would persist even if the ClockSync stream issue were fixed.

1. Revert or fix linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, Jun 23 2026). The liveGate drain-on-reset behavior discards the Ptp4lConf: startup lines that GetProfileLogID depends on. Either: (a) emit a synthetic replay line for slave/follower profiles (matching what EmitClockSyncLogs does for T-GM), or (b) gate the drain only on a confirmed-live line rather than discarding all buffered lines on profile apply. 2. Fix the pod log stream context deadline exceeded disconnects. GetPodLogsRegex should reconnect and resume scanning on stream error rather than propagating the error up through GetProfileLogID. Adding retry-with-reconnect inside findRegexInStream would make the test resilient to the ~60 s kube-apiserver/HAProxy connection timeout independently of the liveGate fix.

3. Investigate the WPC T-GM DPLL holdover configuration. Audit holdoverParameters.maxInSpecOffset in the deployed HardwareConfig for cnfdf32; a value of 20 ns causes the in-spec timer to expire almost immediately. bc


Now I have enough to write the complete analysis. Here it is:



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three bc-serial ClockSync tests fail because GetProfileLogID cannot find the daemon's Ptp4lConf: #profile: startup log within its 3-minute window, most likely because the daemon pods restarted (triggered by the operator-pod-deletion test that ran 9 seconds earlier) and hadn't yet completed initialization when the ClockSync tests began.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Daemon log lookup timeout — GetProfileLogID exhausts its 3-minute search window (affects: all 3 tests)

• Evidence:
• [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master [Serial]: "time": 207.094107648, "result": "error"
• [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]: "time": 206.698651671, "result": "error"
• [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Can provide a profile with higher priority [Serial]: "time": 271.33427173, "result": "error"
• The immediately preceding test logs: [It] [bc-serial] PTP configuration verifications Should re-acquire lease within 5 minutes after operator pod deletion [Serial] passed in 5.23s — this deleted and recreated the ptp-operator pod at ~04:03:09 UTC.
• TimeoutIn3Minutes = 3 * time.Minute (test/pkg/consts.go:50) is the timeout used by GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:81).
• test/pkg/pods/pods.go:232: snapCtx, snapCancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), pkg.TimeoutIn1Minute) — Pass 1 (no-follow existing-log read).
• test/pkg/pods/pods.go:254: ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout) — Pass 2 (follow stream, 3-min limit).
• The bc suite XML header:
• Daemon log rotation baseline: at ~300 bytes/sec over 1h55m ≈ 2 MB, well within the 10 MB CRI-O default — so the startup logs should have been present for the first two tests, but the daemon was mid-restart from the operator reconciliation, so the fresh pod had no buffered Ptp4lConf: entries yet.
• test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:69–93: GetProfileLogID searches for (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: %s(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.*)\] in pod logs; returns error "could not get any profile line" after Pass 2 timeout.
• Timing breakdown: tests 1 & 2 take 207 s ≈ 180 s (Pass 2 timeout) + ~27 s (BeforeEach, findMatchingPod, Pass 1 no-match read). Test 3 takes 271 s ≈ 91 s (WaitForConfigMapProfile in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:386) + 180 s (GetPodLogsRegex waiting for "Profile Name: "+pkg.PtpTempPolicyName).

• Root Cause: BasicClockSyncCheck (test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:32) calls GetClockIDForeign → GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:69), which uses a two-pass GetPodLogsRegex: Pass 1 reads all existing logs (1-minute limit) for Ptp4lConf: #profile: …message_tag [...]; Pass 2 follows the stream for up to 3 minutes. Both pass fail because the linuxptp-daemon pods restarted after the operator reconciliation triggered by the "



Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.21-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 4.21 | Total failures: 15



Cross-Suite Summary


TL;DR: A linuxptp-daemon regression blocks all slave sync-state detection, timing out 13/15 ClockSync tests across every suite.



Root Cause Groups


• 13/15 failures — GetProfileLogID / log-stream timeout blocking ClockSync verification (suites: bc ×3, dualfollower ×3, dualnicbc ×3, dualnicbcha ×2, oc ×2)

Every "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", and "Can provide a profile with higher priority" test hits the same ~207–210 s timeout. Two overlapping mechanisms contribute:


1. liveGate startup drain (dualfollower / all slave suites): The new liveGate mechanism in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go blocks the socket-writer goroutine for up to 60 s after each profile apply and then discards buffered ptp4l lines. EmitClockSyncLogs() only replays state for T-GM nodes, so slave/follower sync-state is invisible to the test framework until a fresh live line arrives — which never happens within the GetProfileLogID 3-minute window.
2. Pod log stream closed with context deadline exceeded (~60–70 s, dualnicbc / bc): The Follow: true Kubernetes log stream is cut by network infrastructure (HAProxy / kube-apiserver ~60 s idle timeout in OCP 4.21 nightly), causing both retry passes of GetProfileLogID to fail before the Eventually expires.

Both mechanisms trace to the same upstream sync merged the day before the run (linuxptp-daemon PR #613, commit 0459bc0, Jun 23 2026).


• 2/15 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL never sustains clock-class 7 holdover on GNSS loss (suite: tgm ×2)

In both TGM holdover tests, the DPLL on cnfdf32 either never reaches class 7 (Test 1: 47+ retries, 197 s timeout) or exits class-7 immediately to class 248/FREERUN (Test 2). Root cause is an overly aggressive maxInSpecOffset (20 ns) in HardwareConfig, causing the in-spec holdover timer to collapse to ~192 s and the DPLL to transition directly to FREERUN before the test verifies it. This is isolated to the tgm suite and the WPC NIC hardware on that node.



Cross-Suite Patterns


SuiteFailuresRoot Cause
bc3/3GetProfileLogID timeout (operator-pod-deletion restarts daemon mid-test)
dualfollower3/3liveGate drains buffered lines; slave state invisible
dualnicbc3/3Log stream closed context deadline exceeded at ~60–70 s
dualnicbcha2/2Same systemic ClockSync timeout (no XML artifact captured)
tgm2/2Suite-specific: DPLL holdover hardware/config regression

Shared: bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha all share the same underlying cause — the linuxptp-daemon liveGate/stream regression introduced in PR #613 prevents GetProfileLogID from ever receiving the Ptp4lConf: #profile: startup log within its timeout window.


Suite-specific: tgm failures are independent — caused by a HardwareConfig holdover parameter misconfiguration (or a DPLL driver regression) on the WPC NIC node; they would persist even if the ClockSync stream issue were fixed.



Actionable Recommendations


1. Revert or fix linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, Jun 23 2026). The liveGate drain-on-reset behavior discards the Ptp4lConf: startup lines that GetProfileLogID depends on. Either: (a) emit a synthetic replay line for slave/follower profiles (matching what EmitClockSyncLogs does for T-GM), or (b) gate the drain only on a confirmed-live line rather than discarding all buffered lines on profile apply. This alone unblocks 13/15 failures.

2. Fix the pod log stream context deadline exceeded disconnects. GetPodLogsRegex should reconnect and resume scanning on stream error rather than propagating the error up through GetProfileLogID. Adding retry-with-reconnect inside findRegexInStream would make the test resilient to the ~60 s kube-apiserver/HAProxy connection timeout independently of the liveGate fix.

3. Investigate the WPC T-GM DPLL holdover configuration. Audit holdoverParameters.maxInSpecOffset in the deployed HardwareConfig for cnfdf32; a value of 20 ns causes the in-spec timer to expire almost immediately. Align it with realistic oscillator specs (e.g., ≥100 ns) or audit the linuxptp-daemon/pkg/dpll/dpll.go holdover-timer formula for a regression introduced alongside PR #613.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 13/15 ClockSync tests fail across every non-tgm suite due to a single code change merged the previous day; the entire periodic 4.21 PTP job is effectively non-functional.




Per-Suite Details



bc

Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.21-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 4.21 | Total failures: 15



Cross-Suite Summary


TL;DR: A linuxptp-daemon regression blocks all slave sync-state detection, timing out 13/15 ClockSync tests across every suite.



Root Cause Groups


• 13/15 failures — GetProfileLogID / log-stream timeout blocking ClockSync verification (suites: bc ×3, dualfollower ×3, dualnicbc ×3, dualnicbcha ×2, oc ×2)

Every "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", and "Can provide a profile with higher priority" test hits the same ~207–210 s timeout. Two overlapping mechanisms contribute:


1. liveGate startup drain (dualfollower / all slave suites): The new liveGate mechanism in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go blocks the socket-writer goroutine for up to 60 s after each profile apply and then discards buffered ptp4l lines. EmitClockSyncLogs() only replays state for T-GM nodes, so slave/follower sync-state is invisible to the test framework until a fresh live line arrives — which never happens within the GetProfileLogID 3-minute window.
2. Pod log stream closed with context deadline exceeded (~60–70 s, dualnicbc / bc): The Follow: true Kubernetes log stream is cut by network infrastructure (HAProxy / kube-apiserver ~60 s idle timeout in OCP 4.21 nightly), causing both retry passes of GetProfileLogID to fail before the Eventually expires.

Both mechanisms trace to the same upstream sync merged the day before the run (linuxptp-daemon PR #613, commit 0459bc0, Jun 23 2026).


• 2/15 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL never sustains clock-class 7 holdover on GNSS loss (suite: tgm ×2)

In both TGM holdover tests, the DPLL on cnfdf32 either never reaches class 7 (Test 1: 47+ retries, 197 s timeout) or exits class-7 immediately to class 248/FREERUN (Test 2). Root cause is an overly aggressive maxInSpecOffset (20 ns) in HardwareConfig, causing the in-spec holdover timer to collapse to ~192 s and the DPLL to transition directly to FREERUN before the test verifies it. This is isolated to the tgm suite and the WPC NIC hardware on that node.



Cross-Suite Patterns


SuiteFailuresRoot Cause
bc3/3GetProfileLogID timeout (operator-pod-deletion restarts daemon mid-test)
dualfollower3/3liveGate drains buffered lines; slave state invisible
dualnicbc3/3Log stream closed context deadline exceeded at ~60–70 s
dualnicbcha2/2Same systemic ClockSync timeout (no XML artifact captured)

Shared: bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha all share the same underlying cause — the linuxptp-daemon liveGate/stream regression introduced in PR #613 prevents GetProfileLogID from ever receiving the Ptp4lConf: #profile: startup log within its timeout window.


Suite-specific: tgm failures are independent — caused by a HardwareConfig holdover parameter misconfiguration (or a DPLL driver regression) on the WPC NIC node; they would persist even if the ClockSync stream issue were fixed.

1. Revert or fix linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, Jun 23 2026). The liveGate drain-on-reset behavior discards the Ptp4lConf: startup lines that GetProfileLogID depends on. Either: (a) emit a synthetic replay line for slave/follower profiles (matching what EmitClockSyncLogs does for T-GM), or (b) gate the drain only on a confirmed-live line rather than discarding all buffered lines on profile apply. This alone unblocks 13/15 failures.

2. Fix the pod log stream context deadline exceeded disconnects. GetPodLogsRegex should reconnect and resume scanning on stream error rather than propagating the error up through GetProfileLogID. Adding retry-with-reconnect inside findRegexInStream would make the test resilient to the ~60 s kube-apiserver/HAProxy connection timeout independently of the liveGate fix.

3. Investigate the WPC T-GM DPLL holdover configuration. Audit holdoverParameters.maxInSpecOffset in the deployed HardwareConfig for cnfdf32; a value of 20 ns causes the in-spec timer to expire almost immediately. Align it with realistic oscillator specs (e.g., ≥100 ns) or audit the linuxptp-daemon/pkg/dpll/dpll.go holdover-timer formula for a regression introduced alongside PR #613.

Now I have everything I need for the analysis. Let me write it out:



Test 1: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down [Serial]


• Duration: 612.78s (~10m12s)
• Error: Timed out after 600.001s. Timeout to detect metrics for ptpconfig test-slave1 — incorrect clock state on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com iface ens7f1: expected HOLDOVER, got LOCKED. Per-interface results: ens7f0: role mismatch (expected=FAULTY, got=SLAVE); ens7f1: clock state mismatch (expected=HOLDOVER, got=LOCKED)
• Root Cause: The test (ptp.go:756, TurnPortDown at ptptesthelper.go:416) runs ip link set ens7f0 down inside the linuxptp-daemon pod on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, then waits up to 120s for PTP to report the port as FAULTY. The port remained SLAVE throughout the entire 600s Eventually window, indicating the NIC or PTP stack on cnfdf32 did not detect or propagate the link-down event. With Port0 still SLAVE, Port1 never entered HOLDOVER. This is a hardware/infra failure — the dual-follower NIC on cnfdf32 (ens7f0/ens7f1) does not respond to software-initiated link-down as expected by the test.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Not a code regression. Most recent relevant change: [PR #224 ccd1e711](https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/ccd1e711) (2026-04-20) replaced toggleNetworkInterface with PortEngine/TurnPortDown, which uses ip link set. The ip link set approach may not trigger a PTP-visible link-down on all NIC models.
• Source File: [https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-5.0/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L756](https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-5.0/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L756)
• Is it a flake? No — the port role never changed at all across 600s; this is a persistent hardware incompatibility, not a timing race.
• Suggested Fix: Verify that node cnfdf32 has PTP-capable NICs that respond to ip link set down with a PTP port state transition to FAULTY. If the NIC (or its driver) does not propagate link-down to the PTP layer, the CI node needs to be replaced or the hardware configuration corrected before this test can pass.


Relation Between Failures: There is only one failing test. The single failure is caused by PTP-capable hardware on node cnfdf32 not reacting to ip link set down with the expected PTP port state change (SLAVE→FAULTY), blocking the HOLDOVER transition. All 146 other tests passed; this is an isolated hardware/infra issue on the dual-follower test node.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 5.0 (release-5.0) — dual-follower PTP test failed due to infra NIC issue on cnfdf32; all other test suites (bc, dualnicbc, parallel) passed cleanly.


Affected Images:

• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:5.0

Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: LOW — only one test failed due to infra hardware behavior; the PTP operator itself and 145 other tests are healthy. The root cause is the test node's NIC not transitioning PTP port state on ip link set down, not a ptp-operator code regression.



Now I have enough information to write the complete analysis.



Test 1: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down [Serial]

• Duration: 715s
• Error: Timeout waiting for CheckClockRoleAndOffset to report MetricClockStateLocked / MetricRoleSlave — BasicClockSyncCheck exceeded its 10-minute TimeoutIn10Minutes limit.
• Root Cause: The dual-follower topology requires two physical PTP-capable follower ports. BasicClockSyncCheck in ptptesthelper.go:105 polls metrics.CheckClockRoleAndOffset for up to 10 minutes; after port manipulation the clock never returned to LOCKED, indicating the dualfollower L2 link is not syncing. Identical test skipped (correctly) in bc-serial, and passes in dualnicbc; only dualfollower hardware is missing/degraded.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — likely infra regression, not code; no ptp-operator source change implicated.
• Source File: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L756
• Is it a flake? No — the 715s duration (close to full 10-min BasicClockSyncCheck timeout) indicates a genuine sync failure, not a transient race.
• Suggested Fix: Verify the physical NIC wiring and interface configuration for the dualfollower topology on nodes cnfdf30–cnfdf32. Check whether both follower ports are connected and the upstream PTP master is reachable on both.


Test 2: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Can provide a profile with higher priority [Serial]

• Duration: 655s
• Error: Timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck while checking sync after applying a temp PtpTempPolicyName profile — clock never reached MetricClockStateLocked within TimeoutIn10Minutes.
• Root Cause: Test at ptp.go:962 calls BasicClockSyncCheck for the modified config in dualfollower mode; because the dualfollower setup is not syncing at all, ptptesthelper.go:99-105 repeatedly polls CheckClockRoleAndOffset and times out. The same test passes in 105s on bc-serial, confirming the operator/profile logic is correct and only dualfollower sync is broken.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown
• Source File: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L892
• Is it a flake? No — the near-full timeout duration (655s ≈ 10m 55s) is consistent with a deterministic infrastructure failure.
• Suggested Fix: Same as Test 1 — restore dualfollower PTP sync connectivity; once the L2 topology is healthy this test will pass without code changes.


Test 3: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP metric is present verifies on slave [Serial]

• Duration: 300s
• Error: Eventually at ptp.go:1265-1269 timed out after TimeoutIn5Minutes (300s) waiting for openshift_ptp_offset_ns metric to appear at the metrics endpoint.
• Root Cause: The test curls the linuxptp-daemon metrics endpoint expecting openshift_ptp_offset_ns to be reported, which only happens once ptp4l achieves sync. Because the dualfollower clock is not syncing (same root cause as Tests 1 & 2), the offset metric is never emitted and the 5-minute Eventually window exhausts exactly. The same test in bc-serial passes in 3.2s.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown
• Source File: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L1261
• Is it a flake? No — exactly hitting the 5-minute timeout boundary indicates the metric was never present, not intermittently missing.
• Suggested Fix: Fixing the dualfollower sync issue (hardware/wiring) will resolve this; no test or code changes needed.


Test 4: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests Running with event enabled Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart [Serial]

• Duration: 187s
• Error: event.PushInitialEvent at ptp.go:1585 timed out (2-minute limit) waiting for a SyncStateChange cloud-event after linuxptp-daemon pod restart via ptphelper.ReplaceTestPod.
• Root Cause: After the daemon pod is restarted, cloud-event-proxy publishes a SyncStateChange event only once ptp4l re-establishes sync. Because the dualfollower topology is not syncing (same underlying cause), the LOCKED state never arrives and the event consumer sees no SyncStateChange within 2 minutes. The same test passes in 74s on bc-serial where sync is healthy.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown; recent cloud-event-proxy PR #700 (Jun 11, 2026) only updates a CI workflow URL, not event-publishing logic.
• Source File: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L1546
• Is it a flake? No — the event is never emitted because PTP sync doesn't complete; this is a deterministic hardware-layer failure.
• Suggested Fix: No code change needed; restoring dualfollower PTP sync will allow cloud-event-proxy to emit the SyncStateChange event as expected.


Relation Between Failures: All four failures share a single root cause — the dualfollower PTP clock configuration on nodes cnfdf30–cnfdf32 is not achieving LOCKED sync state. Tests 1, 2, and 3 fail because BasicClockSyncCheck / metrics polling time out waiting for offset metrics in the dualfollower topology. Test 4 is a downstream effect: cloud-event-proxy only emits SyncStateChange once ptp4l syncs, which never happens in this run.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 5.0 (nightly-5.0): all 4 failures observed only in [dualfollower-serial] suite; bc-serial and dualnicbc-serial test suites on the same nodes pass completely.


Affected Images:

• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:5.0

Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: MEDIUM — the dualfollower-specific topology is broken on this lab cluster; bc and dualnicbc tests continue to pass, so the PTP operator itself is functioning correctly. Failures are deterministic and will recur on every run until the dualfollower L2 link/hardware is remediated.




Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.21-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 4.21 | Total failures: 15



Cross-Suite Summary


TL;DR: A linuxptp-daemon regression blocks all slave sync-state detection, timing out 13/15 ClockSync tests across every suite.



Root Cause Groups


• 13/15 failures — GetProfileLogID / log-stream timeout blocking ClockSync verification (suites: bc ×3, dualfollower ×3, dualnicbc ×3, dualnicbcha ×2, oc ×2)

Every "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", and "Can provide a profile with higher priority" test hits the same ~207–210 s timeout. Two overlapping mechanisms contribute:


1. liveGate startup drain (dualfollower / all slave suites): The new liveGate mechanism in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go blocks the socket-writer goroutine for up to 60 s after each profile apply and then discards buffered ptp4l lines. EmitClockSyncLogs() only replays state for T-GM nodes, so slave/follower sync-state is invisible to the test framework until a fresh live line arrives — which never happens within the GetProfileLogID 3-minute window.
2. Pod log stream closed with context deadline exceeded (~60–70 s, dualnicbc / bc): The Follow: true Kubernetes log stream is cut by network infrastructure (HAProxy / kube-apiserver ~60 s idle timeout in OCP 4.21 nightly), causing both retry passes of GetProfileLogID to fail before the Eventually expires.

Both mechanisms trace to the same upstream sync merged the day before the run (linuxptp-daemon PR #613, commit 0459bc0, Jun 23 2026).


• 2/15 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL never sustains clock-class 7 holdover on GNSS loss (suite: tgm ×2)

In both TGM holdover tests, the DPLL on cnfdf32 either never reaches class 7 (Test 1: 47+ retries, 197 s timeout) or exits class-7 immediately to class 248/FREERUN (Test 2). Root cause is an overly aggressive maxInSpecOffset (20 ns) in HardwareConfig, causing the in-spec holdover timer to collapse to ~192 s and the DPLL to transition directly to FREERUN before the test verifies it. This is isolated to the tgm suite and the WPC NIC hardware on that node.



Cross-Suite Patterns


SuiteFailuresRoot Cause
bc3/3GetProfileLogID timeout (operator-pod-deletion restarts daemon mid-test)
dualfollower3/3liveGate drains buffered lines; slave state invisible
dualnicbc3/3Log stream closed context deadline exceeded at ~60–70 s
dualnicbcha2/2Same systemic ClockSync timeout (no XML artifact captured)
tgm2/2Suite-specific: DPLL holdover hardware/config regression

Shared: bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha all share the same underlying cause — the linuxptp-daemon liveGate/stream regression introduced in PR #613 prevents GetProfileLogID from ever receiving the Ptp4lConf: #profile: startup log within its timeout window.


Suite-specific: tgm failures are independent — caused by a HardwareConfig holdover parameter misconfiguration (or a DPLL driver regression) on the WPC NIC node; they would persist even if the ClockSync stream issue were fixed.



Actionable Recommendations


1. Revert or fix linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, Jun 23 2026). The liveGate drain-on-reset behavior discards the Ptp4lConf: startup lines that GetProfileLogID depends on. Either: (a) emit a synthetic replay line for slave/follower profiles (matching what EmitClockSyncLogs does for T-GM), or (b) gate the drain only on a confirmed-live line rather than discarding all buffered lines on profile apply. This alone unblocks 13/15 failures.

2. Fix the pod log stream context deadline exceeded disconnects. GetPodLogsRegex should reconnect and resume scanning on stream error rather than propagating the error up through GetProfileLogID. Adding retry-with-reconnect inside findRegexInStream would make the test resilient to the ~60 s kube-apiserver/HAProxy connection timeout independently of the liveGate fix.

3. Investigate the WPC T-GM DPLL holdover configuration. Audit holdoverParameters.maxInSpecOffset in the deployed HardwareConfig for cnfdf32; a value of 20 ns causes the in-spec timer to expire almost immediately. Align it with realistic oscillator specs (e.g., ≥100 ns) or audit the linuxptp-daemon/pkg/dpll/dpll.go holdover-timer formula for a regression introduced alongside PR #613.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 13/15 ClockSync tests fail across every non-tgm suite due to a single code change merged the previous day; the entire periodic 4.21 PTP job is effectively non-functional.




Per-Suite Details



bc


Now I have enough to write the complete analysis. Here it is:



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three bc-serial ClockSync tests fail because GetProfileLogID cannot find the daemon's Ptp4lConf: #profile: startup log within its 3-minute window, most likely because the daemon pods restarted (triggered by the operator-pod-deletion test that ran 9 seconds earlier) and hadn't yet completed initialization when the ClockSync tests began.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Daemon log lookup timeout — GetProfileLogID exhausts its 3-minute search window (affects: all 3 tests)

• Evidence:
• [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master [Serial]: "time": 207.094107648, "result": "error"
• [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]: "time": 206.698651671, "result": "error"
• [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Can provide a profile with higher priority [Serial]: "time": 271.33427173, "result": "error"
• The immediately preceding test logs: [It] [bc-serial] PTP configuration verifications Should re-acquire lease within 5 minutes after operator pod deletion [Serial] passed in 5.23s — this deleted and recreated the ptp-operator pod at ~04:03:09 UTC.
• TimeoutIn3Minutes = 3 * time.Minute (test/pkg/consts.go:50) is the timeout used by GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:81).
• test/pkg/pods/pods.go:232: snapCtx, snapCancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), pkg.TimeoutIn1Minute) — Pass 1 (no-follow existing-log read).
• test/pkg/pods/pods.go:254: ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout) — Pass 2 (follow stream, 3-min limit).
• The bc suite XML header:
• Daemon log rotation baseline: at ~300 bytes/sec over 1h55m ≈ 2 MB, well within the 10 MB CRI-O default — so the startup logs should have been present for the first two tests, but the daemon was mid-restart from the operator reconciliation, so the fresh pod had no buffered Ptp4lConf: entries yet.
• test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:69–93: GetProfileLogID searches for (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: %s(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.*)\] in pod logs; returns error "could not get any profile line" after Pass 2 timeout.
• Timing breakdown: tests 1 & 2 take 207 s ≈ 180 s (Pass 2 timeout) + ~27 s (BeforeEach, findMatchingPod, Pass 1 no-match read). Test 3 takes 271 s ≈ 91 s (WaitForConfigMapProfile in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:386) + 180 s (GetPodLogsRegex waiting for "Profile Name: "+pkg.PtpTempPolicyName).

• Root Cause: BasicClockSyncCheck (test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:32) calls GetClockIDForeign → GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:69), which uses a two-pass GetPodLogsRegex: Pass 1 reads all existing logs (1-minute limit) for Ptp4lConf: #profile: …message_tag [...]; Pass 2 follows the stream for up to 3 minutes. Both pass fail because the linuxptp-daemon pods restarted after the operator reconciliation triggered by the "



Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.21-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 4.21 | Total failures: 15



Cross-Suite Summary


TL;DR: A linuxptp-daemon regression blocks all slave sync-state detection, timing out 13/15 ClockSync tests across every suite.



Root Cause Groups


• 13/15 failures — GetProfileLogID / log-stream timeout blocking ClockSync verification (suites: bc ×3, dualfollower ×3, dualnicbc ×3, dualnicbcha ×2, oc ×2)

Every "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", and "Can provide a profile with higher priority" test hits the same ~207–210 s timeout. Two overlapping mechanisms contribute:


1. liveGate startup drain (dualfollower / all slave suites): The new liveGate mechanism in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go blocks the socket-writer goroutine for up to 60 s after each profile apply and then discards buffered ptp4l lines. EmitClockSyncLogs() only replays state for T-GM nodes, so slave/follower sync-state is invisible to the test framework until a fresh live line arrives — which never happens within the GetProfileLogID 3-minute window.
2. Pod log stream closed with context deadline exceeded (~60–70 s, dualnicbc / bc): The Follow: true Kubernetes log stream is cut by network infrastructure (HAProxy / kube-apiserver ~60 s idle timeout in OCP 4.21 nightly), causing both retry passes of GetProfileLogID to fail before the Eventually expires.

Both mechanisms trace to the same upstream sync merged the day before the run (linuxptp-daemon PR #613, commit 0459bc0, Jun 23 2026).


• 2/15 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL never sustains clock-class 7 holdover on GNSS loss (suite: tgm ×2)

In both TGM holdover tests, the DPLL on cnfdf32 either never reaches class 7 (Test 1: 47+ retries, 197 s timeout) or exits class-7 immediately to class 248/FREERUN (Test 2). Root cause is an overly aggressive maxInSpecOffset (20 ns) in HardwareConfig, causing the in-spec holdover timer to collapse to ~192 s and the DPLL to transition directly to FREERUN before the test verifies it. This is isolated to the tgm suite and the WPC NIC hardware on that node.



Cross-Suite Patterns


SuiteFailuresRoot Cause
bc3/3GetProfileLogID timeout (operator-pod-deletion restarts daemon mid-test)
dualfollower3/3liveGate drains buffered lines; slave state invisible
dualnicbc3/3Log stream closed context deadline exceeded at ~60–70 s
dualnicbcha2/2Same systemic ClockSync timeout (no XML artifact captured)
tgm2/2Suite-specific: DPLL holdover hardware/config regression

Shared: bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha all share the same underlying cause — the linuxptp-daemon liveGate/stream regression introduced in PR #613 prevents GetProfileLogID from ever receiving the Ptp4lConf: #profile: startup log within its timeout window.


Suite-specific: tgm failures are independent — caused by a HardwareConfig holdover parameter misconfiguration (or a DPLL driver regression) on the WPC NIC node; they would persist even if the ClockSync stream issue were fixed.



Actionable Recommendations


1. Revert or fix linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, Jun 23 2026). The liveGate drain-on-reset behavior discards the Ptp4lConf: startup lines that GetProfileLogID depends on. Either: (a) emit a synthetic replay line for slave/follower profiles (matching what EmitClockSyncLogs does for T-GM), or (b) gate the drain only on a confirmed-live line rather than discarding all buffered lines on profile apply. This alone unblocks 13/15 failures.

2. Fix the pod log stream context deadline exceeded disconnects. GetPodLogsRegex should reconnect and resume scanning on stream error rather than propagating the error up through GetProfileLogID. Adding retry-with-reconnect inside findRegexInStream would make the test resilient to the ~60 s kube-apiserver/HAProxy connection timeout independently of the liveGate fix.

3. Investigate the WPC T-GM DPLL holdover configuration. Audit holdoverParameters.maxInSpecOffset in the deployed HardwareConfig for cnfdf32; a value of 20 ns causes the in-spec timer to expire almost immediately. Align it with realistic oscillator specs (e.g., ≥100 ns) or audit the linuxptp-daemon/pkg/dpll/dpll.go holdover-timer formula for a regression introduced alongside PR #613.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 13/15 ClockSync tests fail across every non-tgm suite due to a single code change merged the previous day; the entire periodic 4.21 PTP job is effectively non-functional.




Per-Suite Details

I now have all the information needed. Let me write the final analysis:



Test 1: [dualnicbcha-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master [Serial]

• Duration: Unknown (evidence truncated; the Eventually block has a pkg.TimeoutIn10Minutes timeout, so likely ~10+ min)
• Error: result: error — BasicClockSyncCheck timed out waiting for MetricClockStateLocked/MetricRoleSlave on the DualNIC BC HA clock under test
• Root Cause: In dualnicbcha mode the test calls ptptesthelper.BasicClockSyncCheck() ([ptptesthelper.go:32](https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-5.0/test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go#L32)), which polls metrics.CheckClockRoleAndOffset with a 10-minute Eventually block. The test did not skip (discovery did not return DiscoveryFailureStatus), yet ptp4l never reached LOCKED state. The cluster was flagged "error state" and triggered must-gather, consistent with the hardware for DualNIC BC HA (two PTP-capable NICs per node connected to an upstream GM) being absent or malfunctioning on the test lab hardware at the time of the run.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — this is an infra/hardware availability issue, not a code regression
• Source File: [openshift/ptp-operator — test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L574](https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-5.0/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L574)
• Is it a flake? Yes — hardware-environment failures (NIC unavailability, lab connectivity issues) are transient and do not reproduce consistently across runs
• Suggested Fix: Verify the dualnicbcha test node has two PTP-capable NICs (e.g., Intel E810) physically cabled to an upstream GM; retry the job after confirming hardware readiness


Relation Between Failures: There is only one failing test. All other test suites (bc, dualnicbc, dualfollower) passed. The single failure is isolated to the dualnicbcha hardware topology; the cluster's lab environment lacked proper dual-NIC PTP hardware at the time of the run, causing a cluster-error-state event (must-gather trigger).


Per-Version Notes: OCP 5.0 — one error in dualnicbcha mode; all other PTP modes (bc, dualnicbc, dualfollower) fully passed (146 pass, 121 skip, 0 fail)


Affected Images:

• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:5.0

Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: LOW

Suite TL;DR: All three bc-serial ClockSync tests fail because GetProfileLogID cannot find the daemon's Ptp4lConf: #profile: startup log within its 3-minute window, most likely because the daemon pods restarted (triggered by the operator-pod-deletion test that ran 9 seconds earlier) and hadn't yet completed initialization when the ClockSync tests began.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Daemon log lookup timeout — GetProfileLogID exhausts its 3-minute search window (affects: all 3 tests)

• Evidence:
• [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master [Serial]: "time": 207.094107648, "result": "error"
• [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]: "time": 206.698651671, "result": "error"
• [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Can provide a profile with higher priority [Serial]: "time": 271.33427173, "result": "error"
• The immediately preceding test logs: [It] [bc-serial] PTP configuration verifications Should re-acquire lease within 5 minutes after operator pod deletion [Serial] passed in 5.23s — this deleted and recreated the ptp-operator pod at ~04:03:09 UTC.
• TimeoutIn3Minutes = 3 * time.Minute (test/pkg/consts.go:50) is the timeout used by GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:81).
• test/pkg/pods/pods.go:232: snapCtx, snapCancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), pkg.TimeoutIn1Minute) — Pass 1 (no-follow existing-log read).
• test/pkg/pods/pods.go:254: ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout) — Pass 2 (follow stream, 3-min limit).
• The bc suite XML header:
• Daemon log rotation baseline: at ~300 bytes/sec over 1h55m ≈ 2 MB, well within the 10 MB CRI-O default — so the startup logs should have been present for the first two tests, but the daemon was mid-restart from the operator reconciliation, so the fresh pod had no buffered Ptp4lConf: entries yet.
• test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:69–93: GetProfileLogID searches for (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: %s(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.*)\] in pod logs; returns error "could not get any profile line" after Pass 2 timeout.
• Timing breakdown: tests 1 & 2 take 207 s ≈ 180 s (Pass 2 timeout) + ~27 s (BeforeEach, findMatchingPod, Pass 1 no-match read). Test 3 takes 271 s ≈ 91 s (WaitForConfigMapProfile in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:386) + 180 s (GetPodLogsRegex waiting for "Profile Name: "+pkg.PtpTempPolicyName).

• Root Cause: BasicClockSyncCheck (test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:32) calls GetClockIDForeign → GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:69), which uses a two-pass GetPodLogsRegex: Pass 1 reads all existing logs (1-minute limit) for Ptp4lConf: #profile: …message_tag [...]; Pass 2 follows the stream for up to 3 minutes. Both pass fail because the linuxptp-daemon pods restarted after the operator reconciliation triggered by the "



Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.21-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 4.21 | Total failures: 15



Cross-Suite Summary


TL;DR: A linuxptp-daemon regression blocks all slave sync-state detection, timing out 13/15 ClockSync tests across every suite.



Root Cause Groups


• 13/15 failures — GetProfileLogID / log-stream timeout blocking ClockSync verification (suites: bc ×3, dualfollower ×3, dualnicbc ×3, dualnicbcha ×2, oc ×2)

Every "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", and "Can provide a profile with higher priority" test hits the same ~207–210 s timeout. Two overlapping mechanisms contribute:


1. liveGate startup drain (dualfollower / all slave suites): The new liveGate mechanism in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go blocks the socket-writer goroutine for up to 60 s after each profile apply and then discards buffered ptp4l lines. EmitClockSyncLogs() only replays state for T-GM nodes, so slave/follower sync-state is invisible to the test framework until a fresh live line arrives — which never happens within the GetProfileLogID 3-minute window.
2. Pod log stream closed with context deadline exceeded (~60–70 s, dualnicbc / bc): The Follow: true Kubernetes log stream is cut by network infrastructure (HAProxy / kube-apiserver ~60 s idle timeout in OCP 4.21 nightly), causing both retry passes of GetProfileLogID to fail before the Eventually expires.

Both mechanisms trace to the same upstream sync merged the day before the run (linuxptp-daemon PR #613, commit 0459bc0, Jun 23 2026).


• 2/15 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL never sustains clock-class 7 holdover on GNSS loss (suite: tgm ×2)

In both TGM holdover tests, the DPLL on cnfdf32 either never reaches class 7 (Test 1: 47+ retries, 197 s timeout) or exits class-7 immediately to class 248/FREERUN (Test 2). Root cause is an overly aggressive maxInSpecOffset (20 ns) in HardwareConfig, causing the in-spec holdover timer to collapse to ~192 s and the DPLL to transition directly to FREERUN before the test verifies it. This is isolated to the tgm suite and the WPC NIC hardware on that node.



Cross-Suite Patterns


SuiteFailuresRoot Cause
bc3/3GetProfileLogID timeout (operator-pod-deletion restarts daemon mid-test)
dualfollower3/3liveGate drains buffered lines; slave state invisible
dualnicbc3/3Log stream closed context deadline exceeded at ~60–70 s
dualnicbcha2/2Same systemic ClockSync timeout (no XML artifact captured)
tgm2/2Suite-specific: DPLL holdover hardware/config regression

Shared: bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha all share the same underlying cause — the linuxptp-daemon liveGate/stream regression introduced in PR #613 prevents GetProfileLogID from ever receiving the Ptp4lConf: #profile: startup log within its timeout window.


Suite-specific: tgm failures are independent — caused by a HardwareConfig holdover parameter misconfiguration (or a DPLL driver regression) on the WPC NIC node; they would persist even if the ClockSync stream issue were fixed.



Actionable Recommendations


1. Revert or fix linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, Jun 23 2026). The liveGate drain-on-reset behavior discards the Ptp4lConf: startup lines that GetProfileLogID depends on. Either: (a) emit a synthetic replay line for slave/follower profiles (matching what EmitClockSyncLogs does for T-GM), or (b) gate the drain only on a confirmed-live line rather than discarding all buffered lines on profile apply. This alone unblocks 13/15 failures.

2. Fix the pod log stream context deadline exceeded disconnects. GetPodLogsRegex should reconnect and resume scanning on stream error rather than propagating the error up through GetProfileLogID. Adding retry-with-reconnect inside findRegexInStream would make the test resilient to the ~60 s kube-apiserver/HAProxy connection timeout independently of the liveGate fix.

3. Investigate the WPC T-GM DPLL holdover configuration. Audit holdoverParameters.maxInSpecOffset in the deployed HardwareConfig for cnfdf32; a value of 20 ns causes the in-spec timer to expire almost immediately. Align it with realistic oscillator specs (e.g., ≥100 ns) or audit the linuxptp-daemon/pkg/dpll/dpll.go holdover-timer formula for a regression introduced alongside PR #613.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 13/15 ClockSync tests fail across every non-tgm suite due to a single code change merged the previous day; the entire periodic 4.21 PTP job is effectively non-functional.




Per-Suite Details



bc

• Root Cause: BasicClockSyncCheck (test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:32) calls GetClockIDForeign → GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:69), which uses a two-pass GetPodLogsRegex: Pass 1 reads all existing logs (1-minute limit) for Ptp4lConf: #profile: …message_tag [...]; Pass 2 follows the stream for up to 3 minutes. Both pass fail because the linuxptp-daemon pods restarted after the operator reconciliation triggered by the "

Version: 4.21 | Total failures: 15



Cross-Suite Summary


TL;DR: A linuxptp-daemon regression blocks all slave sync-state detection, timing out 13/15 ClockSync tests across every suite.



Root Cause Groups


• 13/15 failures — GetProfileLogID / log-stream timeout blocking ClockSync verification (suites: bc ×3, dualfollower ×3, dualnicbc ×3, dualnicbcha ×2, oc ×2)

Every "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", and "Can provide a profile with higher priority" test hits the same ~207–210 s timeout. Two overlapping mechanisms contribute:


1. liveGate startup drain (dualfollower / all slave suites): The new liveGate mechanism in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go blocks the socket-writer goroutine for up to 60 s after each profile apply and then discards buffered ptp4l lines. EmitClockSyncLogs() only replays state for T-GM nodes, so slave/follower sync-state is invisible to the test framework until a fresh live line arrives — which never happens within the GetProfileLogID 3-minute window.
2. Pod log stream closed with context deadline exceeded (~60–70 s, dualnicbc / bc): The Follow: true Kubernetes log stream is cut by network infrastructure (HAProxy / kube-apiserver ~60 s idle timeout in OCP 4.21 nightly), causing both retry passes of GetProfileLogID to fail before the Eventually expires.

Both mechanisms trace to the same upstream sync merged the day before the run (linuxptp-daemon PR #613, commit 0459bc0, Jun 23 2026).


• 2/15 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL never sustains clock-class 7 holdover on GNSS loss (suite: tgm ×2)

In both TGM holdover tests, the DPLL on cnfdf32 either never reaches class 7 (Test 1: 47+ retries, 197 s timeout) or exits class-7 immediately to class 248/FREERUN (Test 2). Root cause is an overly aggressive maxInSpecOffset (20 ns) in HardwareConfig, causing the in-spec holdover timer to collapse to ~192 s and the DPLL to transition directly to FREERUN before the test verifies it. This is isolated to the tgm suite and the WPC NIC hardware on that node.

I have all the evidence needed. Here is the final analysis:



TL;DR: GNSS receiver on WPC T-GM lab node cnfdf32 never achieved satellite lock, so ptp4l stayed at clockClass 248 instead of 6.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GNSS hardware failure on WPC T-GM node (cnfdf32) (affects all 5 tests)
• Evidence:

```

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:18Z gnss[..] ens7f0 gnss_status -1 offset -1 s0

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:36Z ptp4l[..] CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE 248

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:37Z event.go:528] dpll State s2, gnss State s0, tsphc state s2, gm state s0

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:09Z gpsd.go:179] stty: /dev/gnss0: Inappropriate ioctl for device

[FAILED] could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-lmlk4 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

• Root Cause: The GNSS receiver (/dev/gnss0) on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com reported gnss_status -1 offset -1 s0 (no fix) from the first ts2phc sample at 13:01:18 through the entire test window. Without GNSS lock (s2), the gm state stays s0, and ptp4l cannot advance past clockClass 248 to the required clockClass 6 (Locked). The stty: Inappropriate ioctl for device at gpsd startup suggests a low-level GNSS device interface fault. In parallel, linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf32 cycled through at least 4 pod instances (4zqzk → 2l8nt → xt6qs → lmlk4) during the test session, causing the log-stream context deadline on the final pod lmlk4 for the first test.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no code change involved; this is a physical hardware / lab environment issue.
• Is it a flake? No — GNSS reported gnss_status -1 for every single second across the full tgm test run; this is a sustained hardware fault, not a transient timing race.
• Suggested Fix: Inspect the GNSS antenna connection and receiver hardware on cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com. Verify /dev/gnss0 is returning valid NMEA sentences and that the antenna has clear sky visibility. Until fixed, the tgm serial test suite cannot pass on this node.
• Affected tests:
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on logs [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on metrics [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM ts2phc termination tests Continuously terminating ts2phc triggers FREERUN event and then recovers to LOCKED [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]


Relation Between Groups: Single group — all 5 failures share the same first domino: the GNSS receiver on cnfdf32 returned gnss_status -1 with no satellite fix, preventing ptp4l from ever reaching clockClass 6. The pod restart loop is a secondary symptom on the same node that additionally caused the log-stream deadline error in test 1.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 4.22 nightly (4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-06-23-025516): all 5 WPC GM tests fail; all other test suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc) passed (142 pass / 0 fail in their suites).


Affected Images: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.22 (git commit 502725f392d851a441df7f502b02dcc743207cb4), cloud-event-proxy:4.22, ptp-operator:4.22 — all deployed successfully; the failure is not in these images.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: MEDIUM



I have all the evidence needed. Here is the final analysis:



TL;DR: GNSS receiver on WPC T-GM lab node cnfdf32 never achieved satellite lock, so ptp4l stayed at clockClass 248 instead of 6.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GNSS hardware failure on WPC T-GM node (cnfdf32) (affects all 5 tests)
• Evidence:

```

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:18Z gnss[..] ens7f0 gnss_status -1 offset -1 s0

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:36Z ptp4l[..] CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE 248

The pod restart loop is a secondary symptom on the same node that additionally caused the log-stream deadline error in test 1.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 4.22 nightly (4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-06-23-025516): all 5 WPC GM tests fail; all other test suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc) passed (142 pass / 0 fail in their suites).


Affected Images: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.22 (git commit 502725f392d851a441df7f502b02dcc743207cb4), cloud-event-proxy:4.22, ptp-operator:4.22 — all deployed successfully; the failure is not in these images.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: MEDIUM

2026-06-24T00:00
❌ failure4.224.22-e2e-telco5g-ptp273minfra_other MEDIUM
GNSS receiver on WPC T-GM lab node cnfdf32 never achieved satellite lock, so ptp4l stayed at clockClass 248 instead of 6.
6 failed tests
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

Test 1: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down [Serial]


• Duration: 612.78s (~10m12s)
• Error: Timed out after 600.001s. Timeout to detect metrics for ptpconfig test-slave1 — incorrect clock state on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com iface ens7f1: expected HOLDOVER, got LOCKED. Per-interface results: ens7f0: role mismatch (expected=FAULTY, got=SLAVE); ens7f1: clock state mismatch (expected=HOLDOVER, got=LOCKED)
• Root Cause: The test (ptp.go:756, TurnPortDown at ptptesthelper.go:416) runs ip link set ens7f0 down inside the linuxptp-daemon pod on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, then waits up to 120s for PTP to report the port as FAULTY. The port remained SLAVE throughout the entire 600s Eventually window, indicating the NIC or PTP stack on cnfdf32 did not detect or propagate the link-down event. With Port0 still SLAVE, Port1 never entered HOLDOVER. This is a hardware/infra failure — the dual-follower NIC on cnfdf32 (ens7f0/ens7f1) does not respond to software-initiated link-down as expected by the test.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Not a code regression. Most recent relevant change: [PR #224 ccd1e711](https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/ccd1e711) (2026-04-20) replaced toggleNetworkInterface with PortEngine/TurnPortDown, which uses ip link set. The ip link set approach may not trigger a PTP-visible link-down on all NIC models.
• Source File: [https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-5.0/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L756](https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-5.0/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L756)
• Is it a flake? No — the port role never changed at all across 600s; this is a persistent hardware incompatibility, not a timing race.
• Suggested Fix: Verify that node cnfdf32 has PTP-capable NICs that respond to ip link set down with a PTP port state transition to FAULTY. If the NIC (or its driver) does not propagate link-down to the PTP layer, the CI node needs to be replaced or the hardware configuration corrected before this test can pass.


Relation Between Failures: There is only one failing test. The single failure is caused by PTP-capable hardware on node cnfdf32 not reacting to ip link set down with the expected PTP port state change (SLAVE→FAULTY), blocking the HOLDOVER transition. All 146 other tests passed; this is an isolated hardware/infra issue on the dual-follower test node.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 5.0 (release-5.0) — dual-follower PTP test failed due to infra NIC issue on cnfdf32; all other test suites (bc, dualnicbc, parallel) passed cleanly.


Affected Images:

• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:5.0

Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: LOW — only one test failed due to infra hardware behavior; the PTP operator itself and 145 other tests are healthy. The root cause is the test node's NIC not transitioning PTP port state on ip link set down, not a ptp-operator code regression.



Now I have enough information to write the complete analysis.



Test 1: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down [Serial]

• Duration: 715s
• Error: Timeout waiting for CheckClockRoleAndOffset to report MetricClockStateLocked / MetricRoleSlave — BasicClockSyncCheck exceeded its 10-minute TimeoutIn10Minutes limit.
• Root Cause: The dual-follower topology requires two physical PTP-capable follower ports. BasicClockSyncCheck in ptptesthelper.go:105 polls metrics.CheckClockRoleAndOffset for up to 10 minutes; after port manipulation the clock never returned to LOCKED, indicating the dualfollower L2 link is not syncing. Identical test skipped (correctly) in bc-serial, and passes in dualnicbc; only dualfollower hardware is missing/degraded.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — likely infra regression, not code; no ptp-operator source change implicated.
• Source File: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L756
• Is it a flake? No — the 715s duration (close to full 10-min BasicClockSyncCheck timeout) indicates a genuine sync failure, not a transient race.
• Suggested Fix: Verify the physical NIC wiring and interface configuration for the dualfollower topology on nodes cnfdf30–cnfdf32. Check whether both follower ports are connected and the upstream PTP master is reachable on both.


Test 2: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Can provide a profile with higher priority [Serial]

• Duration: 655s
• Error: Timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck while checking sync after applying a temp PtpTempPolicyName profile — clock never reached MetricClockStateLocked within TimeoutIn10Minutes.
• Root Cause: Test at ptp.go:962 calls BasicClockSyncCheck for the modified config in dualfollower mode; because the dualfollower setup is not syncing at all, ptptesthelper.go:99-105 repeatedly polls CheckClockRoleAndOffset and times out. The same test passes in 105s on bc-serial, confirming the operator/profile logic is correct and only dualfollower sync is broken.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown
• Source File: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L892
• Is it a flake? No — the near-full timeout duration (655s ≈ 10m 55s) is consistent with a deterministic infrastructure failure.
• Suggested Fix: Same as Test 1 — restore dualfollower PTP sync connectivity; once the L2 topology is healthy this test will pass without code changes.


Test 3: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP metric is present verifies on slave [Serial]

• Duration: 300s
• Error: Eventually at ptp.go:1265-1269 timed out after TimeoutIn5Minutes (300s) waiting for openshift_ptp_offset_ns metric to appear at the metrics endpoint.
• Root Cause: The test curls the linuxptp-daemon metrics endpoint expecting openshift_ptp_offset_ns to be reported, which only happens once ptp4l achieves sync. Because the dualfollower clock is not syncing (same root cause as Tests 1 & 2), the offset metric is never emitted and the 5-minute Eventually window exhausts exactly. The same test in bc-serial passes in 3.2s.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown
• Source File: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L1261
• Is it a flake? No — exactly hitting the 5-minute timeout boundary indicates the metric was never present, not intermittently missing.
• Suggested Fix: Fixing the dualfollower sync issue (hardware/wiring) will resolve this; no test or code changes needed.


Test 4: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests Running with event enabled Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart [Serial]

• Duration: 187s
• Error: event.PushInitialEvent at ptp.go:1585 timed out (2-minute limit) waiting for a SyncStateChange cloud-event after linuxptp-daemon pod restart via ptphelper.ReplaceTestPod.
• Root Cause: After the daemon pod is restarted, cloud-event-proxy publishes a SyncStateChange event only once ptp4l re-establishes sync. Because the dualfollower topology is not syncing (same underlying cause), the LOCKED state never arrives and the event consumer sees no SyncStateChange within 2 minutes. The same test passes in 74s on bc-serial where sync is healthy.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown; recent cloud-event-proxy PR #700 (Jun 11, 2026) only updates a CI workflow URL, not event-publishing logic.
• Source File: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L1546
• Is it a flake? No — the event is never emitted because PTP sync doesn't complete; this is a deterministic hardware-layer failure.
• Suggested Fix: No code change needed; restoring dualfollower PTP sync will allow cloud-event-proxy to emit the SyncStateChange event as expected.


Relation Between Failures: All four failures share a single root cause — the dualfollower PTP clock configuration on nodes cnfdf30–cnfdf32 is not achieving LOCKED sync state. Tests 1, 2, and 3 fail because BasicClockSyncCheck / metrics polling time out waiting for offset metrics in the dualfollower topology. Test 4 is a downstream effect: cloud-event-proxy only emits SyncStateChange once ptp4l syncs, which never happens in this run.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 5.0 (nightly-5.0): all 4 failures observed only in [dualfollower-serial] suite; bc-serial and dualnicbc-serial test suites on the same nodes pass completely.


Affected Images:

• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:5.0

Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: MEDIUM — the dualfollower-specific topology is broken on this lab cluster; bc and dualnicbc tests continue to pass, so the PTP operator itself is functioning correctly. Failures are deterministic and will recur on every run until the dualfollower L2 link/hardware is remediated.



I have all the evidence needed. Here is the final analysis:



TL;DR: GNSS receiver on WPC T-GM lab node cnfdf32 never achieved satellite lock, so ptp4l stayed at clockClass 248 instead of 6.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GNSS hardware failure on WPC T-GM node (cnfdf32) (affects all 5 tests)
• Evidence:

```

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:18Z gnss[..] ens7f0 gnss_status -1 offset -1 s0

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:36Z ptp4l[..] CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE 248

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:37Z event.go:528] dpll State s2, gnss State s0, tsphc state s2, gm state s0

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:09Z gpsd.go:179] stty: /dev/gnss0: Inappropriate ioctl for device

[FAILED] could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-lmlk4 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

• Root Cause: The GNSS receiver (/dev/gnss0) on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com reported gnss_status -1 offset -1 s0 (no fix) from the first ts2phc sample at 13:01:18 through the entire test window. Without GNSS lock (s2), the gm state stays s0, and ptp4l cannot advance past clockClass 248 to the required clockClass 6 (Locked). The stty: Inappropriate ioctl for device at gpsd startup suggests a low-level GNSS device interface fault. In parallel, linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf32 cycled through at least 4 pod instances (4zqzk → 2l8nt → xt6qs → lmlk4) during the test session, causing the log-stream context deadline on the final pod lmlk4 for the first test.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no code change involved; this is a physical hardware / lab environment issue.
• Is it a flake? No — GNSS reported gnss_status -1 for every single second across the full tgm test run; this is a sustained hardware fault, not a transient timing race.
• Suggested Fix: Inspect the GNSS antenna connection and receiver hardware on cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com. Verify /dev/gnss0 is returning valid NMEA sentences and that the antenna has clear sky visibility. Until fixed, the tgm serial test suite cannot pass on this node.
• Affected tests:
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on logs [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on metrics [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM ts2phc termination tests Continuously terminating ts2phc triggers FREERUN event and then recovers to LOCKED [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]


Relation Between Groups: Single group — all 5 failures share the same first domino: the GNSS receiver on cnfdf32 returned gnss_status -1 with no satellite fix, preventing ptp4l from ever reaching clockClass 6. The pod restart loop is a secondary symptom on the same node that additionally caused the log-stream deadline error in test 1.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 4.22 nightly (4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-06-23-025516): all 5 WPC GM tests fail; all other test suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc) passed (142 pass / 0 fail in their suites).


Affected Images: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.22 (git commit 502725f392d851a441df7f502b02dcc743207cb4), cloud-event-proxy:4.22, ptp-operator:4.22 — all deployed successfully; the failure is not in these images.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: MEDIUM

Test 1: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down [Serial]

• Duration: 715s

• Root Cause: The dual-follower topology requires two physical PTP-capable follower ports. BasicClockSyncCheck in ptptesthelper.go:105 polls metrics.CheckClockRoleAndOffset for up to 10 minutes; after port manipulation the clock never returned to LOCKED, indicating the dualfollower L2 link is not syncing. Identical test skipped (correctly) in bc-serial, and passes in dualnicbc; only dualfollower hardware is missing/degraded.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — likely infra regression, not code; no ptp-operator source change implicated.
• Source File: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L756
• Is it a flake? No — the 715s duration (close to full 10-min BasicClockSyncCheck timeout) indicates a genuine sync failure, not a transient race.
• Suggested Fix: Verify the physical NIC wiring and interface configuration for the dualfollower topology on nodes cnfdf30–cnfdf32. Check whether both follower ports are connected and the upstream PTP master is reachable on both.


Test 2: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Can provide a profile with higher priority [Serial]

• Duration: 655s
• Error: Timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck while checking sync after applying a temp PtpTempPolicyName profile — clock never reached MetricClockStateLocked within TimeoutIn10Minutes.
• Root Cause: Test at ptp.go:962 calls BasicClockSyncCheck for the modified config in dualfollower mode; because the dualfollower setup is not syncing at all, ptptesthelper.go:99-105 repeatedly polls CheckClockRoleAndOffset and times out. The same test passes in 105s on bc-serial, confirming the operator/profile logic is correct and only dualfollower sync is broken.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown
• Source File: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L892
• Is it a flake? No — the near-full timeout duration (655s ≈ 10m 55s) is consistent with a deterministic infrastructure failure.
• Suggested Fix: Same as Test 1 — restore dualfollower PTP sync connectivity; once the L2 topology is healthy this test will pass without code changes.


Test 3: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP metric is present verifies on slave [Serial]

• Duration: 300s
• Error: Eventually at ptp.go:1265-1269 timed out after TimeoutIn5Minutes (300s) waiting for openshift_ptp_offset_ns metric to appear at the metrics endpoint.
• Root Cause: The test curls the linuxptp-daemon metrics endpoint expecting openshift_ptp_offset_ns to be reported, which only happens once ptp4l achieves sync. Because the dualfollower clock is not syncing (same root cause as Tests 1 & 2), the offset metric is never emitted and the 5-minute Eventually window exhausts exactly. The same test in bc-serial passes in 3.2s.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown
• Source File: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L1261
• Is it a flake? No — exactly hitting the 5-minute timeout boundary indicates the metric was never present, not intermittently missing.
• Suggested Fix: Fixing the dualfollower sync issue (hardware/wiring) will resolve this; no test or code changes needed.


Test 4: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests Running with event enabled Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart [Serial]

• Duration: 187s
• Error: event.PushInitialEvent at ptp.go:1585 timed out (2-minute limit) waiting for a SyncStateChange cloud-event after linuxptp-daemon pod restart via ptphelper.ReplaceTestPod.
• Root Cause: After the daemon pod is restarted, cloud-event-proxy publishes a SyncStateChange event only once ptp4l re-establishes sync. Because the dualfollower topology is not syncing (same underlying cause), the LOCKED state never arrives and the event consumer sees no SyncStateChange within 2 minutes. The same test passes in 74s on bc-serial where sync is healthy.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown; recent cloud-event-proxy PR #700 (Jun 11, 2026) only updates a CI workflow URL, not event-publishing logic.
• Source File: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/blob/main/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L1546
• Is it a flake? No — the event is never emitted because PTP sync doesn't complete; this is a deterministic hardware-layer failure.
• Suggested Fix: No code change needed; restoring dualfollower PTP sync will allow cloud-event-proxy to emit the SyncStateChange event as expected.


Relation Between Failures: All four failures share a single root cause — the dualfollower PTP clock configuration on nodes cnfdf30–cnfdf32 is not achieving LOCKED sync state. Tests 1, 2, and 3 fail because BasicClockSyncCheck / metrics polling time out waiting for offset metrics in the dualfollower topology. Test 4 is a downstream effect: cloud-event-proxy only emits SyncStateChange once ptp4l syncs, which never happens in this run.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 5.0 (nightly-5.0): all 4 failures observed only in [dualfollower-serial] suite; bc-serial and dualnicbc-serial test suites on the same nodes pass completely.


Affected Images:

• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:5.0

Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: MEDIUM — the dualfollower-specific topology is broken on this lab cluster; bc and dualnicbc tests continue to pass, so the PTP operator itself is functioning correctly. Failures are deterministic and will recur on every run until the dualfollower L2 link/hardware is remediated.
