Full AI Analysis

Test 1: [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down [Serial]


• Duration: 612.78s (~10m12s)
• Error: Timed out after 600.001s. Timeout to detect metrics for ptpconfig test-slave1 — incorrect clock state on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com iface ens7f1: expected HOLDOVER, got LOCKED. Per-interface results: ens7f0: role mismatch (expected=FAULTY, got=SLAVE); ens7f1: clock state mismatch (expected=HOLDOVER, got=LOCKED)
• Root Cause: The test (ptp.go:756, TurnPortDown at ptptesthelper.go:416) runs ip link set ens7f0 down inside the linuxptp-daemon pod on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, then waits up to 120s for PTP to report the port as FAULTY. The port remained SLAVE throughout the entire 600s Eventually window, indicating the NIC or PTP stack on cnfdf32 did not detect or propagate the link-down event. With Port0 still SLAVE, Port1 never entered HOLDOVER. This is a hardware/infra failure — the dual-follower NIC on cnfdf32 (ens7f0/ens7f1) does not respond to software-initiated link-down as expected by the test.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Not a code regression. Most recent relevant change: [PR #224 ccd1e711](https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/commit/ccd1e711) (2026-04-20) replaced toggleNetworkInterface with PortEngine/TurnPortDown, which uses ip link set. The ip link set approach may not trigger a PTP-visible link-down on all NIC models.
• Source File: [https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-5.0/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L756](https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/blob/release-5.0/test/conformance/serial/ptp.go#L756)
• Is it a flake? No — the port role never changed at all across 600s; this is a persistent hardware incompatibility, not a timing race.
• Suggested Fix: Verify that node cnfdf32 has PTP-capable NICs that respond to ip link set down with a PTP port state transition to FAULTY. If the NIC (or its driver) does not propagate link-down to the PTP layer, the CI node needs to be replaced or the hardware configuration corrected before this test can pass.


Relation Between Failures: There is only one failing test. The single failure is caused by PTP-capable hardware on node cnfdf32 not reacting to ip link set down with the expected PTP port state change (SLAVE→FAULTY), blocking the HOLDOVER transition. All 146 other tests passed; this is an isolated hardware/infra issue on the dual-follower test node.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 5.0 (release-5.0) — dual-follower PTP test failed due to infra NIC issue on cnfdf32; all other test suites (bc, dualnicbc, parallel) passed cleanly.


Affected Images:

• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:5.0
• image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:5.0

Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: LOW — only one test failed due to infra hardware behavior; the PTP operator itself and 145 other tests are healthy. The root cause is the test node's NIC not transitioning PTP port state on ip link set down, not a ptp-operator code regression.



I have all the evidence needed. Here is the final analysis:



TL;DR: GNSS receiver on WPC T-GM lab node cnfdf32 never achieved satellite lock, so ptp4l stayed at clockClass 248 instead of 6.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GNSS hardware failure on WPC T-GM node (cnfdf32) (affects all 5 tests)
• Evidence:

```

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:18Z gnss[..] ens7f0 gnss_status -1 offset -1 s0

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:36Z ptp4l[..] CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE 248

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:37Z event.go:528] dpll State s2, gnss State s0, tsphc state s2, gm state s0

[linuxptp-daemon-xt6qs] 2026-06-23T13:01:09Z gpsd.go:179] stty: /dev/gnss0: Inappropriate ioctl for device

[FAILED] could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-lmlk4 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

• Root Cause: The GNSS receiver (/dev/gnss0) on node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com reported gnss_status -1 offset -1 s0 (no fix) from the first ts2phc sample at 13:01:18 through the entire test window. Without GNSS lock (s2), the gm state stays s0, and ptp4l cannot advance past clockClass 248 to the required clockClass 6 (Locked). The stty: Inappropriate ioctl for device at gpsd startup suggests a low-level GNSS device interface fault. In parallel, linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf32 cycled through at least 4 pod instances (4zqzk → 2l8nt → xt6qs → lmlk4) during the test session, causing the log-stream context deadline on the final pod lmlk4 for the first test.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no code change involved; this is a physical hardware / lab environment issue.
• Is it a flake? No — GNSS reported gnss_status -1 for every single second across the full tgm test run; this is a sustained hardware fault, not a transient timing race.
• Suggested Fix: Inspect the GNSS antenna connection and receiver hardware on cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com. Verify /dev/gnss0 is returning valid NMEA sentences and that the antenna has clear sky visibility. Until fixed, the tgm serial test suite cannot pass on this node.
• Affected tests:
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on logs [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on metrics [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM ts2phc termination tests Continuously terminating ts2phc triggers FREERUN event and then recovers to LOCKED [Serial]
• [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]


Relation Between Groups: Single group — all 5 failures share the same first domino: the GNSS receiver on cnfdf32 returned gnss_status -1 with no satellite fix, preventing ptp4l from ever reaching clockClass 6. The pod restart loop is a secondary symptom on the same node that additionally caused the log-stream deadline error in test 1.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 4.22 nightly (4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-06-23-025516): all 5 WPC GM tests fail; all other test suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc) passed (142 pass / 0 fail in their suites).


Affected Images: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.22 (git commit 502725f392d851a441df7f502b02dcc743207cb4), cloud-event-proxy:4.22, ptp-operator:4.22 — all deployed successfully; the failure is not in these images.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: MEDIUM

Overall Issue Class: infra_other

2026-06-24T12:30
❌ failure4.234.23-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream165minfra_other LOW
All 147 PTP tests passed; job failed because post-phase gather steps got Unauthorized from the API server.
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

PROJECT TESTS PASSED — failure is from CI framework


The telco5g-ptp-tests step completed with "passed":true,"result":"SUCCESS" (finished.json) and all 147 executed tests passed (0 failures, 0 errors per test_results.json). No PTP-operator or linuxptp-daemon test failed.


The job-level failure originated in the post phase, specifically in the gather-extra and gather-audit-logs CI steps, which could not authenticate to the cluster API server. Verbatim evidence from ci-operator-build-log.txt:



error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)
error completing cluster type inspection: error running backup collection: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials
error getting cluster version: Unauthorized
ClusterVersion: Installing "" for <unknown>: <unknown>
error: creating temp namespace: Unauthorized
Step e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream-gather-audit-logs failed after 11s.
Step phase post failed after 15m7s.

The cluster API server returned Unauthorized for every request the gather containers made (oc adm inspect, log fetching, namespace creation). This caused both post-step containers to exit with code 1, which ci-operator converted into a job-level failure (executing_graph:step_failed:executing_multi_stage_test). The suspicious ClusterVersion: Installing "" for suggests the cluster may have been in a partially degraded or re-installing state by the time the post phase ran, invalidating the CI service-account credentials used by the gather steps.



TL;DR: All 147 PTP tests passed; job failed because post-phase gather steps got Unauthorized from the API server.


Relation Between Failures: Both gather-extra and gather-audit-logs failed for exactly the same reason — the cluster API server was returning Unauthorized to the CI service account at the time the post phase executed (≈07:30–07:45 UTC). Neither failure has any connection to PTP-operator code; they are sequential post-steps that both hit the same expired/invalidated credential state.


Per-Version Notes: OCP 4.23 nightly (4.23.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-065515) — cluster API auth degraded in post-phase; all project tests passed on this nightly build.


Affected Images: registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/release:4.23.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-065515 (cluster under test); ci/telco-runner:latest (gather step runner). No PTP-operator or linuxptp-daemon images were affected.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: LOW

2026-06-21T05:00
⚠️ Test Failures
❌ failure5.05.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp372mtest_regression CRITICAL
The WPC T-GM never achieved GNSS lock (clock class 248 throughout), cascading all 25 failures across every suite.
25 failed tests
TEST MEDIUM
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

Cross-Suite Summary — periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp


TL;DR: The WPC T-GM never achieved GNSS lock (clock class 248 throughout), cascading all 25 failures across every suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all downstream sync assertions

All tgm (5), all dualfollower (3), and the majority of dualnicbc (≥6) and dualnicbcha (≥6) failures trace back to the WPC T-GM pod linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never advancing ts2phc/ptp4l to clock class 6 (GNSS LOCKED). With no locked grandmaster, every BC and slave node remained in LISTENING or FREERUN, causing 600-second Eventually timeouts on clock-state metrics that linuxptp-daemon only publishes after a port exits LISTENING state. The overall job also hit the 3h45m CI wall-clock timeout.


• 5/25 failures — pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET argument-splitting bug in conformance test helper

Three dualnicbc tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and two dualnicbcha tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master") failed with bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. The pmc binary received the two-word command as two separate argv tokens and rejected each. This is an independent code regression in the ptp-operator conformance test helper's pmc invocation path — it would cause failures even if the GM were locked.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN (clock class 248)All 3~6/9~6/8All 5
pmc arg-split bug—3/92/8—

• The GM FREERUN pattern is fully cross-suite — every suite has at least one failure directly asserting Expected : 248 to equal : 6. This rules out a per-suite or per-node flake.
• The pmc argument bug is isolated to the BC suites (dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) because only those suites issue GET PARENT_DATA_SET queries to identify parent clock identity.
• dualfollower and tgm have no suite-specific causes — both are purely downstream victims of the upstream GM failure.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Investigate the T-GM GNSS lock failure first (highest leverage — blocks ~80% of failures). Check whether the rdu2 lab GNSS antenna/signal was degraded during the 2026-06-21 run window, and compare the ts2phc and linuxptp-daemon behavior between the last passing nightly and 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64). Focus on pkg/daemon/daemon.go NMEA source handling and pkg/parser/ts2phc_parser.go holdover-exit logic for regressions on the new RHEL 10-based kernel.

2. Fix the pmc invocation in the conformance test helper (eliminates 5 deterministic failures). In the ptp-operator test code path that calls pmc for GetForeignClockID/GetBCMasterClockID (around test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:610), pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument string — exec.Command("pmc", "-u", "-b", "0", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET") — rather than two separate tokens. Alternatively, pipe via stdin: echo "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" | pmc -u -b 0 -f .

3. Add a pre-suite GM health gate. Before any BC or follower suite begins, assert that the T-GM reports clock class 6 within a reasonable timeout (e.g., 5 minutes). If the GM is not locked, skip or abort the dependent suites with a clear infrastructure error rather than accumulating hundreds of seconds of timeouts per test.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(Primary: likely ts2phc/linuxptp-daemon regression on new RHCOS 10.2 kernel preventing GNSS lock; Secondary: confirmed code regression in pmc invocation path in the conformance test binary.)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(25/25 failures, 4/4 suites affected, job hit wall-clock timeout; no PTP synchronization validated in this nightly run.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out because the slave interfaces on cnfdf31 never achieved LOCKED state — the WPC TelcoGrandMaster itself was stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), so no downstream device could synchronize.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GM not locked → follower interfaces stuck in LISTENING/FREERUN (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Test 1 & 2 (ptpconfig test-slave1):

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1 (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test", slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]


Test 3 (ptpconfig temp):

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]


From tgm suite (same cluster run, same job), confirming GM state:

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

Expected : false to be true

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


From ci-operator-build-log.txt — test step killed by overall timeout:

{"msg":"Process did not finish before 3h45m0s timeout","time":"2026-06-21T18:25:13Z"}

error: failed to execute wrapped command: exit status 127

```

• Root Cause: The WPC TelcoGrandMaster node remained in FREERUN state (ptp4l clock class 248, confirmed by simultaneous tgm-suite failures) throughout the entire test run. With the upstream GM not locked, ptp4l on the dualfollower slave node cnfdf31 could not proceed past LISTENING state on interfaces ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3. Consequently, the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never published by linuxptp-daemon (the daemon only emits this metric after ptp4l parses a clock-state event, which requires the port to advance past LISTENING — see pkg/metrics/exporter.go:UpdateClockStateMetrics and pkg/event/event.go:971). For ens2f3np3, the openshift_ptp_interface_role metric existed but showed LISTENING (role 5) rather than SLAVE (role 1). This cascade failure — GM FREERUN → slaves stuck in LISTENING → no LOCKED metrics — caused all three 600-second Eventually timeouts in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:93.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the TGM failure (clock class 248) is the upstream blocker. The cluster ran OCP 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64, first RHEL 10-based RHCOS); ts2phc or the WPC-GM linuxptp integration may have a compatibility regression on this kernel. All 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) fail simultaneously, confirming a cluster-wide rather than suite-specific issue. The dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suites additionally show bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from pmc, which is consistent with a linuxptp version incompatibility on RHCOS 10.2 (pmc interactive mode may behave differently on the new kernel/linuxptp build bundled in the 5.0 nightly). Likely narrowed to a regression in the release-5.0 ptp-operator Dockerfile's bundled linuxptp against RHCOS 10.2 kernel 6.12.
• Is it a flake? No — the simultaneous failure of all 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) on the same job run, all showing GM or synchronization failure, indicates a systemic regression rather than a transient flake.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the ts2phc / WPC-GM linuxptp binary built for release-5.0 operates correctly on RHCOS 10.2 (kernel 6.12); check if the OCP 5.0 nightly from 2026-06-20 introduced a kernel or hardware driver change t



Cross-Suite Summary — periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp


TL;DR: The WPC T-GM never achieved GNSS lock (clock class 248 throughout), cascading all 25 failures across every suite.

• 20/25 failures — GM stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all downstream sync assertions

The overall job also hit the 3h45m CI wall-clock timeout.

• Root Cause: The WPC TelcoGrandMaster node remained in FREERUN state (ptp4l clock class 248, confirmed by simultaneous tgm-suite failures) throughout the entire test run. With the upstream GM not locked, ptp4l on the dualfollower slave node cnfdf31 could not proceed past LISTENING state on interfaces ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3. Consequently, the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never published by linuxptp-daemon (the daemon only emits this metric after ptp4l parses a clock-state event, which requires the port to advance past LISTENING — see pkg/metrics/exporter.go:UpdateClockStateMetrics and pkg/event/event.go:971). For ens2f3np3, the openshift_ptp_interface_role metric existed but showed LISTENING (role 5) rather than SLAVE (role 1). This cascade failure — GM FREERUN → slaves stuck in LISTENING → no LOCKED metrics — caused all three 600-second Eventually timeouts in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:93.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the TGM failure (clock class 248) is the upstream blocker. The cluster ran OCP 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64, first RHEL 10-based RHCOS); ts2phc or the WPC-GM linuxptp integration may have a compatibility regression on this kernel. All 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) fail simultaneously, confirming a cluster-wide rather than suite-specific issue. The dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suites additionally show bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from pmc, which is consistent with a linuxptp version incompatibility on RHCOS 10.2 (pmc interactive mode may behave differently on the new kernel/linuxptp build bundled in the 5.0 nightly). Likely narrowed to a regression in the release-5.0 ptp-operator Dockerfile's bundled linuxptp against RHCOS 10.2 kernel 6.12.
• Is it a flake? No — the simultaneous failure of all 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) on the same job run, all showing GM or synchronization failure, indicates a systemic regression rather than a transient flake.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the ts2phc / WPC-GM linuxptp binary built for release-5.0 operates correctly on RHCOS 10.2 (kernel 6.12); check if the OCP 5.0 nightly from 2026-06-20 introduced a kernel or hardware driver change t



Cross-Suite Summary — periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp


TL;DR: The WPC T-GM never achieved GNSS lock (clock class 248 throughout), cascading all 25 failures across every suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all downstream sync assertions

All tgm (5), all dualfollower (3), and the majority of dualnicbc (≥6) and dualnicbcha (≥6) failures trace back to the WPC T-GM pod linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never advancing ts2phc/ptp4l to clock class 6 (GNSS LOCKED). With no locked grandmaster, every BC and slave node remained in LISTENING or FREERUN, causing 600-second Eventually timeouts on clock-state metrics that linuxptp-daemon only publishes after a port exits LISTENING state. The overall job also hit the 3h45m CI wall-clock timeout.


• 5/25 failures — pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET argument-splitting bug in conformance test helper

Three dualnicbc tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and two dualnicbcha tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master") failed with bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. The pmc binary received the two-word command as two separate argv tokens and rejected each. This is an independent code regression in the ptp-operator conformance test helper's pmc invocation path — it would cause failures even if the GM were locked.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN (clock class 248)All 3~6/9~6/8All 5
pmc arg-split bug—3/92/8—

• The GM FREERUN pattern is fully cross-suite — every suite has at least one failure directly asserting Expected : 248 to equal : 6. This rules out a per-suite or per-node flake.
• The pmc argument bug is isolated to the BC suites (dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) because only those suites issue GET PARENT_DATA_SET queries to identify parent clock identity.
• dualfollower and tgm have no suite-specific causes — both are purely downstream victims of the upstream GM failure.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Investigate the T-GM GNSS lock failure first (highest leverage — blocks ~80% of failures). Check whether the rdu2 lab GNSS antenna/signal was degraded during the 2026-06-21 run window, and compare the ts2phc and linuxptp-daemon behavior between the last passing nightly and 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64). Focus on pkg/daemon/daemon.go NMEA source handling and pkg/parser/ts2phc_parser.go holdover-exit logic for regressions on the new RHEL 10-based kernel.

2. Fix the pmc invocation in the conformance test helper (eliminates 5 deterministic failures). In the ptp-operator test code path that calls pmc for GetForeignClockID/GetBCMasterClockID (around test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:610), pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument string — exec.Command("pmc", "-u", "-b", "0", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET") — rather than two separate tokens. Alternatively, pipe via stdin: echo "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" | pmc -u -b 0 -f .

3. Add a pre-suite GM health gate. Before any BC or follower suite begins, assert that the T-GM reports clock class 6 within a reasonable timeout (e.g., 5 minutes). If the GM is not locked, skip or abort the dependent suites with a clear infrastructure error rather than accumulating hundreds of seconds of timeouts per test.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(Primary: likely ts2phc/linuxptp-daemon regression on new RHCOS 10.2 kernel preventing GNSS lock; Secondary: confirmed code regression in pmc invocation path in the conformance test binary.)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(25/25 failures, 4/4 suites affected, job hit wall-clock timeout; no PTP synchronization validated in this nightly run.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out because the slave interfaces on cnfdf31 never achieved LOCKED state — the WPC TelcoGrandMaster itself was stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), so no downstream device could synchronize.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GM not locked → follower interfaces stuck in LISTENING/FREERUN (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Test 1 & 2 (ptpconfig test-slave1):

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1 (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test", slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]


Test 3 (ptpconfig temp):

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]


From tgm suite (same cluster run, same job), confirming GM state:

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

Expected : false to be true

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


From ci-operator-build-log.txt — test step killed by overall timeout:

{"msg":"Process did not finish before 3h45m0s timeout","time":"2026-06-21T18:25:13Z"}

error: failed to execute wrapped command: exit status 127

```

• Root Cause: The WPC TelcoGrandMaster node remained in FREERUN state (ptp4l clock class 248, confirmed by simultaneous tgm-suite failures) throughout the entire test run. With the upstream GM not locked, ptp4l on the dualfollower slave node cnfdf31 could not proceed past LISTENING state on interfaces ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3. Consequently, the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never published by linuxptp-daemon (the daemon only emits this metric after ptp4l parses a clock-state event, which requires the port to advance past LISTENING — see pkg/metrics/exporter.go:UpdateClockStateMetrics and pkg/event/event.go:971). For ens2f3np3, the openshift_ptp_interface_role metric existed but showed LISTENING (role 5) rather than SLAVE (role 1). This cascade failure — GM FREERUN → slaves stuck in LISTENING → no LOCKED metrics — caused all three 600-second Eventually timeouts in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:93.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the TGM failure (clock class 248) is the upstream blocker. The cluster ran OCP 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64, first RHEL 10-based RHCOS); ts2phc or the WPC-GM linuxptp integration may have a compatibility regression on this kernel. All 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) fail simultaneously, confirming a cluster-wide rather than suite-specific issue. The dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suites additionally show bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from pmc, which is consistent with a linuxptp version incompatibility on RHCOS 10.2 (pmc interactive mode may behave differently on the new kernel/linuxptp build bundled in the 5.0 nightly). Likely narrowed to a regression in the release-5.0 ptp-operator Dockerfile's bundled linuxptp against RHCOS 10.2 kernel 6.12.
• Is it a flake? No — the simultaneous failure of all 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) on the same job run, all showing GM or synchronization failure, indicates a systemic regression rather than a transient flake.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the ts2phc / WPC-GM linuxptp binary built for release-5.0 operates correctly on RHCOS 10.2 (kernel 6.12); check if the OCP 5.0 nightly from 2026-06-20 introduced a kernel or hardware driver change t

• The GM FREERUN pattern is fully cross-suite — every suite has at least one failure directly asserting Expected : 248 to equal : 6. This rules out a per-suite or per-node flake.
• The pmc argument bug is isolated to the BC suites (dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) because only those suites issue GET PARENT_DATA_SET queries to identify parent clock identity.
• dualfollower and tgm have no suite-specific causes — both are purely downstream victims of the upstream GM failure.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Investigate the T-GM GNSS lock failure first (highest leverage — blocks ~80% of failures). Check whether the rdu2 lab GNSS antenna/signal was degraded during the 2026-06-21 run window, and compare the ts2phc and linuxptp-daemon behavior between the last passing nightly and 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64). Focus on pkg/daemon/daemon.go NMEA source handling and pkg/parser/ts2phc_parser.go holdover-exit logic for regressions on the new RHEL 10-based kernel.

2. Fix the pmc invocation in the conformance test helper (eliminates 5 deterministic failures). In the ptp-operator test code path that calls pmc for GetForeignClockID/GetBCMasterClockID (around test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:610), pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument string — exec.Command("pmc", "-u", "-b", "0", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET") — rather than two separate tokens. Alternatively, pipe via stdin: echo "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" | pmc -u -b 0 -f .

3. Add a pre-suite GM health gate. Before any BC or follower suite begins, assert that the T-GM reports clock class 6 within a reasonable timeout (e.g., 5 minutes). If the GM is not locked, skip or abort the dependent suites with a clear infrastructure error rather than accumulating hundreds of seconds of timeouts per test.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(Primary: likely ts2phc/linuxptp-daemon regression on new RHCOS 10.2 kernel preventing GNSS lock; Secondary: confirmed code regression in pmc invocation path in the conformance test binary.)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(25/25 failures, 4/4 suites affected, job hit wall-clock timeout; no PTP synchronization validated in this nightly run.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out because the slave interfaces on cnfdf31 never achieved LOCKED state — the WPC TelcoGrandMaster itself was stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), so no downstream device could synchronize.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GM not locked → follower interfaces stuck in LISTENING/FREERUN (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Test 1 & 2 (ptpconfig test-slave1):

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1 (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test", slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]


Test 3 (ptpconfig temp):

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]


From tgm suite (same cluster run, same job), confirming GM state:

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

Expected : false to be true

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


From ci-operator-build-log.txt — test step killed by overall timeout:

{"msg":"Process did not finish before 3h45m0s timeout","time":"2026-06-21T18:25:13Z"}

error: failed to execute wrapped command: exit status 127

```

• Root Cause: The WPC TelcoGrandMaster node remained in FREERUN state (ptp4l clock class 248, confirmed by simultaneous tgm-suite failures) throughout the entire test run. With the upstream GM not locked, ptp4l on the dualfollower slave node cnfdf31 could not proceed past LISTENING state on interfaces ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3. Consequently, the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never published by linuxptp-daemon (the daemon only emits this metric after ptp4l parses a clock-state event, which requires the port to advance past LISTENING — see pkg/metrics/exporter.go:UpdateClockStateMetrics and pkg/event/event.go:971). For ens2f3np3, the openshift_ptp_interface_role metric existed but showed LISTENING (role 5) rather than SLAVE (role 1). This cascade failure — GM FREERUN → slaves stuck in LISTENING → no LOCKED metrics — caused all three 600-second Eventually timeouts in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:93.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the TGM failure (clock class 248) is the upstream blocker. The cluster ran OCP 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64, first RHEL 10-based RHCOS); ts2phc or the WPC-GM linuxptp integration may have a compatibility regression on this kernel. All 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) fail simultaneously, confirming a cluster-wide rather than suite-specific issue. The dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suites additionally show bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from pmc, which is consistent with a linuxptp version incompatibility on RHCOS 10.2 (pmc interactive mode may behave differently on the new kernel/linuxptp build bundled in the 5.0 nightly). Likely narrowed to a regression in the release-5.0 ptp-operator Dockerfile's bundled linuxptp against RHCOS 10.2 kernel 6.12.
• Is it a flake? No — the simultaneous failure of all 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) on the same job run, all showing GM or synchronization failure, indicates a systemic regression rather than a transient flake.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the ts2phc / WPC-GM linuxptp binary built for release-5.0 operates correctly on RHCOS 10.2 (kernel 6.12); check if the OCP 5.0 nightly from 2026-06-20 introduced a kernel or hardware driver change t



Cross-Suite Summary — periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp


TL;DR: The WPC T-GM never achieved GNSS lock (clock class 248 throughout), cascading all 25 failures across every suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all downstream sync assertions

All tgm (5), all dualfollower (3), and the majority of dualnicbc (≥6) and dualnicbcha (≥6) failures trace back to the WPC T-GM pod linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never advancing ts2phc/ptp4l to clock class 6 (GNSS LOCKED). With no locked grandmaster, every BC and slave node remained in LISTENING or FREERUN, causing 600-second Eventually timeouts on clock-state metrics that linuxptp-daemon only publishes after a port exits LISTENING state. The overall job also hit the 3h45m CI wall-clock timeout.


• 5/25 failures — pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET argument-splitting bug in conformance test helper

Three dualnicbc tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and two dualnicbcha tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master") failed with bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. The pmc binary received the two-word command as two separate argv tokens and rejected each. This is an independent code regression in the ptp-operator conformance test helper's pmc invocation path — it would cause failures even if the GM were locked.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN (clock class 248)All 3~6/9~6/8All 5
pmc arg-split bug—3/92/8—

• The GM FREERUN pattern is fully cross-suite — every suite has at least one failure directly asserting Expected : 248 to equal : 6. This rules out a per-suite or per-node flake.
• The pmc argument bug is isolated to the BC suites (dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) because only those suites issue GET PARENT_DATA_SET queries to identify parent clock identity.
• dualfollower and tgm have no suite-specific causes — both are purely downstream victims of the upstream GM failure.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Investigate the T-GM GNSS lock failure first (highest leverage — blocks ~80% of failures). Check whether the rdu2 lab GNSS antenna/signal was degraded during the 2026-06-21 run window, and compare the ts2phc and linuxptp-daemon behavior between the last passing nightly and 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64). Focus on pkg/daemon/daemon.go NMEA source handling and pkg/parser/ts2phc_parser.go holdover-exit logic for regressions on the new RHEL 10-based kernel.

2. Fix the pmc invocation in the conformance test helper (eliminates 5 deterministic failures). In the ptp-operator test code path that calls pmc for GetForeignClockID/GetBCMasterClockID (around test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:610), pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument string — exec.Command("pmc", "-u", "-b", "0", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET") — rather than two separate tokens. Alternatively, pipe via stdin: echo "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" | pmc -u -b 0 -f .

3. Add a pre-suite GM health gate. Before any BC or follower suite begins, assert that the T-GM reports clock class 6 within a reasonable timeout (e.g., 5 minutes). If the GM is not locked, skip or abort the dependent suites with a clear infrastructure error rather than accumulating hundreds of seconds of timeouts per test.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(Primary: likely ts2phc/linuxptp-daemon regression on new RHCOS 10.2 kernel preventing GNSS lock; Secondary: confirmed code regression in pmc invocation path in the conformance test binary.)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(25/25 failures, 4/4 suites affected, job hit wall-clock timeout; no PTP synchronization validated in this nightly run.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower

• Group 1: GM not locked → follower interfaces stuck in LISTENING/FREERUN (affects: all 3 tests)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]

From tgm suite (same cluster run, same job), confirming GM state:

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

Expected : false to be true

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


From ci-operator-build-log.txt — test step killed by overall timeout:

{"msg":"Process did not finish before 3h45m0s timeout","time":"2026-06-21T18:25:13Z"}

error: failed to execute wrapped command: exit status 127

```

• Root Cause: The WPC TelcoGrandMaster node remained in FREERUN state (ptp4l clock class 248, confirmed by simultaneous tgm-suite failures) throughout the entire test run. With the upstream GM not locked, ptp4l on the dualfollower slave node cnfdf31 could not proceed past LISTENING state on interfaces ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3. Consequently, the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never published by linuxptp-daemon (the daemon only emits this metric after ptp4l parses a clock-state event, which requires the port to advance past LISTENING — see pkg/metrics/exporter.go:UpdateClockStateMetrics and pkg/event/event.go:971). For ens2f3np3, the openshift_ptp_interface_role metric existed but showed LISTENING (role 5) rather than SLAVE (role 1). • Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the TGM failure (clock class 248) is the upstream blocker. The cluster ran OCP 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64, first RHEL 10-based RHCOS); ts2phc or the WPC-GM linuxptp integration may have a compatibility regression on this kernel. All 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) fail simultaneously, confirming a cluster-wide rather than suite-specific issue. The dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suites additionally show bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from pmc, which is consistent with a linuxptp version incompatibility on RHCOS 10.2 (pmc interactive mode may behave differently on the new kernel/linuxptp build bundled in the 5.0 nightly). Likely narrowed to a regression in the release-5.0 ptp-operator Dockerfile's bundled linuxptp against RHCOS 10.2 kernel 6.12.
• Is it a flake? No — the simultaneous failure of all 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) on the same job run, all showing GM or synchronization failure, indicates a systemic regression rather than a transient flake.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the ts2phc / WPC-GM linuxptp binary built for release-5.0 operates correctly on RHCOS 10.2 (kernel 6.12); check if the OCP 5.0 nightly from 2026-06-20 introduced a kernel or hardware driver change t

• Root Cause: The WPC TelcoGrandMaster node remained in FREERUN state (ptp4l clock class 248, confirmed by simultaneous tgm-suite failures) throughout the entire test run. With the upstream GM not locked, ptp4l on the dualfollower slave node cnfdf31 could not proceed past LISTENING state on interfaces ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3. Consequently, the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never published by linuxptp-daemon (the daemon only emits this metric after ptp4l parses a clock-state event, which requires the port to advance past LISTENING — see pkg/metrics/exporter.go:UpdateClockStateMetrics and pkg/event/event.go:971). For ens2f3np3, the openshift_ptp_interface_role metric existed but showed LISTENING (role 5) rather than SLAVE (role 1). This cascade failure — GM FREERUN → slaves stuck in LISTENING → no LOCKED metrics — caused all three 600-second Eventually timeouts in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:93.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the TGM failure (clock class 248) is the upstream blocker. The cluster ran OCP 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64, first RHEL 10-based RHCOS); ts2phc or the WPC-GM linuxptp integration may have a compatibility regression on this kernel. All 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) fail simultaneously, confirming a cluster-wide rather than suite-specific issue. The dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suites additionally show bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from pmc, which is consistent with a linuxptp version incompatibility on RHCOS 10.2 (pmc interactive mode may behave differently on the new kernel/linuxptp build bundled in the 5.0 nightly). Likely narrowed to a regression in the release-5.0 ptp-operator Dockerfile's bundled linuxptp against RHCOS 10.2 kernel 6.12.
• Is it a flake? No — the simultaneous failure of all 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) on the same job run, all showing GM or synchronization failure, indicates a systemic regression rather than a transient flake.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the ts2phc / WPC-GM linuxptp binary built for release-5.0 operates correctly on RHCOS 10.2 (kernel 6.12); check if the OCP 5.0 nightly from 2026-06-20 introduced a kernel or hardware driver change t



Cross-Suite Summary — periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp


TL;DR: The WPC T-GM never achieved GNSS lock (clock class 248 throughout), cascading all 25 failures across every suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all downstream sync assertions

All tgm (5), all dualfollower (3), and the majority of dualnicbc (≥6) and dualnicbcha (≥6) failures trace back to the WPC T-GM pod linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never advancing ts2phc/ptp4l to clock class 6 (GNSS LOCKED). With no locked grandmaster, every BC and slave node remained in LISTENING or FREERUN, causing 600-second Eventually timeouts on clock-state metrics that linuxptp-daemon only publishes after a port exits LISTENING state. The overall job also hit the 3h45m CI wall-clock timeout.


• 5/25 failures — pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET argument-splitting bug in conformance test helper

Three dualnicbc tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and two dualnicbcha tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master") failed with bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. The pmc binary received the two-word command as two separate argv tokens and rejected each. This is an independent code regression in the ptp-operator conformance test helper's pmc invocation path — it would cause failures even if the GM were locked.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN (clock class 248)All 3~6/9~6/8All 5
pmc arg-split bug—3/92/8—

• The GM FREERUN pattern is fully cross-suite — every suite has at least one failure directly asserting Expected : 248 to equal : 6. This rules out a per-suite or per-node flake.
• The pmc argument bug is isolated to the BC suites (dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) because only those suites issue GET PARENT_DATA_SET queries to identify parent clock identity.
• dualfollower and tgm have no suite-specific causes — both are purely downstream victims of the upstream GM failure.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Investigate the T-GM GNSS lock failure first (highest leverage — blocks ~80% of failures). Check whether the rdu2 lab GNSS antenna/signal was degraded during the 2026-06-21 run window, and compare the ts2phc and linuxptp-daemon behavior between the last passing nightly and 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64). Focus on pkg/daemon/daemon.go NMEA source handling and pkg/parser/ts2phc_parser.go holdover-exit logic for regressions on the new RHEL 10-based kernel.

2. Fix the pmc invocation in the conformance test helper (eliminates 5 deterministic failures). In the ptp-operator test code path that calls pmc for GetForeignClockID/GetBCMasterClockID (around test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:610), pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument string — exec.Command("pmc", "-u", "-b", "0", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET") — rather than two separate tokens. Alternatively, pipe via stdin: echo "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" | pmc -u -b 0 -f .

3. Add a pre-suite GM health gate. Before any BC or follower suite begins, assert that the T-GM reports clock class 6 within a reasonable timeout (e.g., 5 minutes). If the GM is not locked, skip or abort the dependent suites with a clear infrastructure error rather than accumulating hundreds of seconds of timeouts per test.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(Primary: likely ts2phc/linuxptp-daemon regression on new RHCOS 10.2 kernel preventing GNSS lock; Secondary: confirmed code regression in pmc invocation path in the conformance test binary.)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(25/25 failures, 4/4 suites affected, job hit wall-clock timeout; no PTP synchronization validated in this nightly run.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out because the slave interfaces on cnfdf31 never achieved LOCKED state — the WPC TelcoGrandMaster itself was stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), so no downstream device could synchronize.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GM not locked → follower interfaces stuck in LISTENING/FREERUN (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Test 1 & 2 (ptpconfig test-slave1):

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1 (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test", slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]


Test 3 (ptpconfig temp):

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]


From tgm suite (same cluster run, same job), confirming GM state:

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

Expected : false to be true

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


From ci-operator-build-log.txt — test step killed by overall timeout:

{"msg":"Process did not finish before 3h45m0s timeout","time":"2026-06-21T18:25:13Z"}

error: failed to execute wrapped command: exit status 127

```

• Root Cause: The WPC TelcoGrandMaster node remained in FREERUN state (ptp4l clock class 248, confirmed by simultaneous tgm-suite failures) throughout the entire test run. With the upstream GM not locked, ptp4l on the dualfollower slave node cnfdf31 could not proceed past LISTENING state on interfaces ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3. Consequently, the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never published by linuxptp-daemon (the daemon only emits this metric after ptp4l parses a clock-state event, which requires the port to advance past LISTENING — see pkg/metrics/exporter.go:UpdateClockStateMetrics and pkg/event/event.go:971). For ens2f3np3, the openshift_ptp_interface_role metric existed but showed LISTENING (role 5) rather than SLAVE (role 1). This cascade failure — GM FREERUN → slaves stuck in LISTENING → no LOCKED metrics — caused all three 600-second Eventually timeouts in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:93.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the TGM failure (clock class 248) is the upstream blocker. The cluster ran OCP 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64, first RHEL 10-based RHCOS); ts2phc or the WPC-GM linuxptp integration may have a compatibility regression on this kernel. All 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) fail simultaneously, confirming a cluster-wide rather than suite-specific issue. The dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suites additionally show bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from pmc, which is consistent with a linuxptp version incompatibility on RHCOS 10.2 (pmc interactive mode may behave differently on the new kernel/linuxptp build bundled in the 5.0 nightly). Likely narrowed to a regression in the release-5.0 ptp-operator Dockerfile's bundled linuxptp against RHCOS 10.2 kernel 6.12.
• Is it a flake? No — the simultaneous failure of all 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) on the same job run, all showing GM or synchronization failure, indicates a systemic regression rather than a transient flake.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the ts2phc / WPC-GM linuxptp binary built for release-5.0 operates correctly on RHCOS 10.2 (kernel 6.12); check if the OCP 5.0 nightly from 2026-06-20 introduced a kernel or hardware driver change t

• The GM FREERUN pattern is fully cross-suite — every suite has at least one failure directly asserting Expected : 248 to equal : 6. This rules out a per-suite or per-node flake.
• The pmc argument bug is isolated to the BC suites (dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) because only those suites issue GET PARENT_DATA_SET queries to identify parent clock identity.
• dualfollower and tgm have no suite-specific causes — both are purely downstream victims of the upstream GM failure.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Investigate the T-GM GNSS lock failure first (highest leverage — blocks ~80% of failures). Check whether the rdu2 lab GNSS antenna/signal was degraded during the 2026-06-21 run window, and compare the ts2phc and linuxptp-daemon behavior between the last passing nightly and 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64). Focus on pkg/daemon/daemon.go NMEA source handling and pkg/parser/ts2phc_parser.go holdover-exit logic for regressions on the new RHEL 10-based kernel.

2. Fix the pmc invocation in the conformance test helper (eliminates 5 deterministic failures). In the ptp-operator test code path that calls pmc for GetForeignClockID/GetBCMasterClockID (around test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:610), pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument string — exec.Command("pmc", "-u", "-b", "0", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET") — rather than two separate tokens. Alternatively, pipe via stdin: echo "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" | pmc -u -b 0 -f .

3. Add a pre-suite GM health gate. Before any BC or follower suite begins, assert that the T-GM reports clock class 6 within a reasonable timeout (e.g., 5 minutes). If the GM is not locked, skip or abort the dependent suites with a clear infrastructure error rather than accumulating hundreds of seconds of timeouts per test.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(Primary: likely ts2phc/linuxptp-daemon regression on new RHCOS 10.2 kernel preventing GNSS lock; Secondary: confirmed code regression in pmc invocation path in the conformance test binary.)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(25/25 failures, 4/4 suites affected, job hit wall-clock timeout; no PTP synchronization validated in this nightly run.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out because the slave interfaces on cnfdf31 never achieved LOCKED state — the WPC TelcoGrandMaster itself was stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), so no downstream device could synchronize.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GM not locked → follower interfaces stuck in LISTENING/FREERUN (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Test 1 & 2 (ptpconfig test-slave1):

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1 (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test", slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]


Test 3 (ptpconfig temp):

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]


From tgm suite (same cluster run, same job), confirming GM state:

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

Expected : false to be true

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


From ci-operator-build-log.txt — test step killed by overall timeout:

{"msg":"Process did not finish before 3h45m0s timeout","time":"2026-06-21T18:25:13Z"}

error: failed to execute wrapped command: exit status 127

```

• Root Cause: The WPC TelcoGrandMaster node remained in FREERUN state (ptp4l clock class 248, confirmed by simultaneous tgm-suite failures) throughout the entire test run. With the upstream GM not locked, ptp4l on the dualfollower slave node cnfdf31 could not proceed past LISTENING state on interfaces ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3. Consequently, the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never published by linuxptp-daemon (the daemon only emits this metric after ptp4l parses a clock-state event, which requires the port to advance past LISTENING — see pkg/metrics/exporter.go:UpdateClockStateMetrics and pkg/event/event.go:971). For ens2f3np3, the openshift_ptp_interface_role metric existed but showed LISTENING (role 5) rather than SLAVE (role 1). This cascade failure — GM FREERUN → slaves stuck in LISTENING → no LOCKED metrics — caused all three 600-second Eventually timeouts in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:93.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the TGM failure (clock class 248) is the upstream blocker. The cluster ran OCP 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64, first RHEL 10-based RHCOS); ts2phc or the WPC-GM linuxptp integration may have a compatibility regression on this kernel. All 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) fail simultaneously, confirming a cluster-wide rather than suite-specific issue. The dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suites additionally show bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from pmc, which is consistent with a linuxptp version incompatibility on RHCOS 10.2 (pmc interactive mode may behave differently on the new kernel/linuxptp build bundled in the 5.0 nightly). Likely narrowed to a regression in the release-5.0 ptp-operator Dockerfile's bundled linuxptp against RHCOS 10.2 kernel 6.12.
• Is it a flake? No — the simultaneous failure of all 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) on the same job run, all showing GM or synchronization failure, indicates a systemic regression rather than a transient flake.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the ts2phc / WPC-GM linuxptp binary built for release-5.0 operates correctly on RHCOS 10.2 (kernel 6.12); check if the OCP 5.0 nightly from 2026-06-20 introduced a kernel or hardware driver change t



Cross-Suite Summary — periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp


TL;DR: The WPC T-GM never achieved GNSS lock (clock class 248 throughout), cascading all 25 failures across every suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all downstream sync assertions

All tgm (5), all dualfollower (3), and the majority of dualnicbc (≥6) and dualnicbcha (≥6) failures trace back to the WPC T-GM pod linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never advancing ts2phc/ptp4l to clock class 6 (GNSS LOCKED). With no locked grandmaster, every BC and slave node remained in LISTENING or FREERUN, causing 600-second Eventually timeouts on clock-state metrics that linuxptp-daemon only publishes after a port exits LISTENING state. The overall job also hit the 3h45m CI wall-clock timeout.


• 5/25 failures — pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET argument-splitting bug in conformance test helper

Three dualnicbc tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and two dualnicbcha tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master") failed with bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. The pmc binary received the two-word command as two separate argv tokens and rejected each. This is an independent code regression in the ptp-operator conformance test helper's pmc invocation path — it would cause failures even if the GM were locked.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN (clock class 248)All 3~6/9~6/8All 5
pmc arg-split bug—3/92/8—

• The GM FREERUN pattern is fully cross-suite — every suite has at least one failure directly asserting Expected : 248 to equal : 6. This rules out a per-suite or per-node flake.
• The pmc argument bug is isolated to the BC suites (dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) because only those suites issue GET PARENT_DATA_SET queries to identify parent clock identity.
• dualfollower and tgm have no suite-specific causes — both are purely downstream victims of the upstream GM failure.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Investigate the T-GM GNSS lock failure first (highest leverage — blocks ~80% of failures). Check whether the rdu2 lab GNSS antenna/signal was degraded during the 2026-06-21 run window, and compare the ts2phc and linuxptp-daemon behavior between the last passing nightly and 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64). Focus on pkg/daemon/daemon.go NMEA source handling and pkg/parser/ts2phc_parser.go holdover-exit logic for regressions on the new RHEL 10-based kernel.

2. Fix the pmc invocation in the conformance test helper (eliminates 5 deterministic failures). In the ptp-operator test code path that calls pmc for GetForeignClockID/GetBCMasterClockID (around test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:610), pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument string — exec.Command("pmc", "-u", "-b", "0", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET") — rather than two separate tokens. Alternatively, pipe via stdin: echo "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" | pmc -u -b 0 -f .

3. Add a pre-suite GM health gate. Before any BC or follower suite begins, assert that the T-GM reports clock class 6 within a reasonable timeout (e.g., 5 minutes). If the GM is not locked, skip or abort the dependent suites with a clear infrastructure error rather than accumulating hundreds of seconds of timeouts per test.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(Primary: likely ts2phc/linuxptp-daemon regression on new RHCOS 10.2 kernel preventing GNSS lock; Secondary: confirmed code regression in pmc invocation path in the conformance test binary.)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(25/25 failures, 4/4 suites affected, job hit wall-clock timeout; no PTP synchronization validated in this nightly run.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower

• Group 1: GM not locked → follower interfaces stuck in LISTENING/FREERUN (affects: all 3 tests)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]

From tgm suite (same cluster run, same job), confirming GM state:

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

Expected : false to be true

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


From ci-operator-build-log.txt — test step killed by overall timeout:

{"msg":"Process did not finish before 3h45m0s timeout","time":"2026-06-21T18:25:13Z"}

error: failed to execute wrapped command: exit status 127

```

• Root Cause: The WPC TelcoGrandMaster node remained in FREERUN state (ptp4l clock class 248, confirmed by simultaneous tgm-suite failures) throughout the entire test run. With the upstream GM not locked, ptp4l on the dualfollower slave node cnfdf31 could not proceed past LISTENING state on interfaces ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3. Consequently, the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never published by linuxptp-daemon (the daemon only emits this metric after ptp4l parses a clock-state event, which requires the port to advance past LISTENING — see pkg/metrics/exporter.go:UpdateClockStateMetrics and pkg/event/event.go:971). For ens2f3np3, the openshift_ptp_interface_role metric existed but showed LISTENING (role 5) rather than SLAVE (role 1). • Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the TGM failure (clock class 248) is the upstream blocker. The cluster ran OCP 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64, first RHEL 10-based RHCOS); ts2phc or the WPC-GM linuxptp integration may have a compatibility regression on this kernel. All 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) fail simultaneously, confirming a cluster-wide rather than suite-specific issue. The dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suites additionally show bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from pmc, which is consistent with a linuxptp version incompatibility on RHCOS 10.2 (pmc interactive mode may behave differently on the new kernel/linuxptp build bundled in the 5.0 nightly). Likely narrowed to a regression in the release-5.0 ptp-operator Dockerfile's bundled linuxptp against RHCOS 10.2 kernel 6.12.
• Is it a flake? No — the simultaneous failure of all 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) on the same job run, all showing GM or synchronization failure, indicates a systemic regression rather than a transient flake.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the ts2phc / WPC-GM linuxptp binary built for release-5.0 operates correctly on RHCOS 10.2 (kernel 6.12); check if the OCP 5.0 nightly from 2026-06-20 introduced a kernel or hardware driver change t

• Root Cause: The WPC TelcoGrandMaster node remained in FREERUN state (ptp4l clock class 248, confirmed by simultaneous tgm-suite failures) throughout the entire test run. With the upstream GM not locked, ptp4l on the dualfollower slave node cnfdf31 could not proceed past LISTENING state on interfaces ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3. Consequently, the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never published by linuxptp-daemon (the daemon only emits this metric after ptp4l parses a clock-state event, which requires the port to advance past LISTENING — see pkg/metrics/exporter.go:UpdateClockStateMetrics and pkg/event/event.go:971). For ens2f3np3, the openshift_ptp_interface_role metric existed but showed LISTENING (role 5) rather than SLAVE (role 1). This cascade failure — GM FREERUN → slaves stuck in LISTENING → no LOCKED metrics — caused all three 600-second Eventually timeouts in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:93.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the TGM failure (clock class 248) is the upstream blocker. The cluster ran OCP 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64, first RHEL 10-based RHCOS); ts2phc or the WPC-GM linuxptp integration may have a compatibility regression on this kernel. All 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) fail simultaneously, confirming a cluster-wide rather than suite-specific issue. The dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suites additionally show bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from pmc, which is consistent with a linuxptp version incompatibility on RHCOS 10.2 (pmc interactive mode may behave differently on the new kernel/linuxptp build bundled in the 5.0 nightly). Likely narrowed to a regression in the release-5.0 ptp-operator Dockerfile's bundled linuxptp against RHCOS 10.2 kernel 6.12.
• Is it a flake? No — the simultaneous failure of all 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) on the same job run, all showing GM or synchronization failure, indicates a systemic regression rather than a transient flake.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the ts2phc / WPC-GM linuxptp binary built for release-5.0 operates correctly on RHCOS 10.2 (kernel 6.12); check if the OCP 5.0 nightly from 2026-06-20 introduced a kernel or hardware driver change t



Cross-Suite Summary — periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp


TL;DR: The WPC T-GM never achieved GNSS lock (clock class 248 throughout), cascading all 25 failures across every suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all downstream sync assertions

All tgm (5), all dualfollower (3), and the majority of dualnicbc (≥6) and dualnicbcha (≥6) failures trace back to the WPC T-GM pod linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never advancing ts2phc/ptp4l to clock class 6 (GNSS LOCKED). With no locked grandmaster, every BC and slave node remained in LISTENING or FREERUN, causing 600-second Eventually timeouts on clock-state metrics that linuxptp-daemon only publishes after a port exits LISTENING state. The overall job also hit the 3h45m CI wall-clock timeout.


• 5/25 failures — pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET argument-splitting bug in conformance test helper

Three dualnicbc tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and two dualnicbcha tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master") failed with bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. The pmc binary received the two-word command as two separate argv tokens and rejected each. This is an independent code regression in the ptp-operator conformance test helper's pmc invocation path — it would cause failures even if the GM were locked.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN (clock class 248)All 3~6/9~6/8All 5
pmc arg-split bug—3/92/8—

• The GM FREERUN pattern is fully cross-suite — every suite has at least one failure directly asserting Expected : 248 to equal : 6. This rules out a per-suite or per-node flake.
• The pmc argument bug is isolated to the BC suites (dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) because only those suites issue GET PARENT_DATA_SET queries to identify parent clock identity.
• dualfollower and tgm have no suite-specific causes — both are purely downstream victims of the upstream GM failure.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Investigate the T-GM GNSS lock failure first (highest leverage — blocks ~80% of failures). Check whether the rdu2 lab GNSS antenna/signal was degraded during the 2026-06-21 run window, and compare the ts2phc and linuxptp-daemon behavior between the last passing nightly and 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64). Focus on pkg/daemon/daemon.go NMEA source handling and pkg/parser/ts2phc_parser.go holdover-exit logic for regressions on the new RHEL 10-based kernel.

2. Fix the pmc invocation in the conformance test helper (eliminates 5 deterministic failures). In the ptp-operator test code path that calls pmc for GetForeignClockID/GetBCMasterClockID (around test/conformance/serial/ptp.go:610), pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument string — exec.Command("pmc", "-u", "-b", "0", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET") — rather than two separate tokens. Alternatively, pipe via stdin: echo "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" | pmc -u -b 0 -f .

3. Add a pre-suite GM health gate. Before any BC or follower suite begins, assert that the T-GM reports clock class 6 within a reasonable timeout (e.g., 5 minutes). If the GM is not locked, skip or abort the dependent suites with a clear infrastructure error rather than accumulating hundreds of seconds of timeouts per test.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(Primary: likely ts2phc/linuxptp-daemon regression on new RHCOS 10.2 kernel preventing GNSS lock; Secondary: confirmed code regression in pmc invocation path in the conformance test binary.)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(25/25 failures, 4/4 suites affected, job hit wall-clock timeout; no PTP synchronization validated in this nightly run.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out because the slave interfaces on cnfdf31 never achieved LOCKED state — the WPC TelcoGrandMaster itself was stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248), so no downstream device could synchronize.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GM not locked → follower interfaces stuck in LISTENING/FREERUN (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Test 1 & 2 (ptpconfig test-slave1):

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1 (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test", slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]


Test 3 (ptpconfig temp):

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state, nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found, available op[...]


From tgm suite (same cluster run, same job), confirming GM state:

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

Expected : false to be true

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


From ci-operator-build-log.txt — test step killed by overall timeout:

{"msg":"Process did not finish before 3h45m0s timeout","time":"2026-06-21T18:25:13Z"}

error: failed to execute wrapped command: exit status 127

```

• Root Cause: The WPC TelcoGrandMaster node remained in FREERUN state (ptp4l clock class 248, confirmed by simultaneous tgm-suite failures) throughout the entire test run. With the upstream GM not locked, ptp4l on the dualfollower slave node cnfdf31 could not proceed past LISTENING state on interfaces ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3. Consequently, the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never published by linuxptp-daemon (the daemon only emits this metric after ptp4l parses a clock-state event, which requires the port to advance past LISTENING — see pkg/metrics/exporter.go:UpdateClockStateMetrics and pkg/event/event.go:971). For ens2f3np3, the openshift_ptp_interface_role metric existed but showed LISTENING (role 5) rather than SLAVE (role 1). This cascade failure — GM FREERUN → slaves stuck in LISTENING → no LOCKED metrics — caused all three 600-second Eventually timeouts in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:93.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the TGM failure (clock class 248) is the upstream blocker. The cluster ran OCP 5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-20-203914 on RHCOS 10.2.20260617-0 (kernel 6.12.0-211.7.1.el10_2.x86_64, first RHEL 10-based RHCOS); ts2phc or the WPC-GM linuxptp integration may have a compatibility regression on this kernel. All 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) fail simultaneously, confirming a cluster-wide rather than suite-specific issue. The dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suites additionally show bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET from pmc, which is consistent with a linuxptp version incompatibility on RHCOS 10.2 (pmc interactive mode may behave differently on the new kernel/linuxptp build bundled in the 5.0 nightly). Likely narrowed to a regression in the release-5.0 ptp-operator Dockerfile's bundled linuxptp against RHCOS 10.2 kernel 6.12.
• Is it a flake? No — the simultaneous failure of all 4 suites (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) on the same job run, all showing GM or synchronization failure, indicates a systemic regression rather than a transient flake.
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the ts2phc / WPC-GM linuxptp binary built for release-5.0 operates correctly on RHCOS 10.2 (kernel 6.12); check if the OCP 5.0 nightly from 2026-06-20 introduced a kernel or hardware driver change t
